Name: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center
Description:
NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center is a major U.S. laboratory for developing and operating unmanned scientific spacecraft. The center manages many of NASA's Earth observation, astronomy, planetary science and astrophysics missions.

NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, is home to the nation's largest organization of scientists, engineers and technologists who build spacecraft, instruments and new technology to study Earth, the sun, our solar system and the universe.

Just outside Washington, Goddard is home to Hubble operations and the upcoming James Webb Space Telescope. Goddard manages communications between mission control and orbiting astronauts aboard the International Space Station. Goddard scientists stare into the sun, grind up meteorites for signs of life's building blocks, look into the farthest reaches of space, and untangle the mysteries of our own changing world. Goddard engineers construct sensitive instruments, build telescopes that peer into the cosmos, and operate the test chambers that ensure those satellites' survival.

Named for American rocketry pioneer Dr. Robert H. Goddard, the center was established May 1, 1959, as NASA's first space flight complex. Goddard and its several installations are critical in carrying out NASA's missions of space exploration and scientific discovery.

Goddard encompasses several other NASA properties, most significantly:

• Wallops Flight Facility near Chincoteague, Virginia
• Katherine Johnson Independent Verification and Validation Facility in Fairmont, West Virginia
• Goddard Institute for Space Studies in New York City
• White Sands Complex outside Las Cruces, New Mexico
• Columbia Scientific Balloon Facility in Palestine, Texas

Name: NAVSEA Warfare Centers (NSWC/NUWC)
Description:
The Naval Surface and Undersea Warfare Centers (NSWC / NUWC) - part of the Naval Sea Systems Command - are the Navy's Innovation Engine.  We are also the Navy's Trusted Technical Experts. We identify and provide innovative, cost-effective technical solutions to America's warfighters. 

Our 27,000 + scientists, engineers, researchers, and technicians work in 10, innovative and diverse Divisions around the country, tackling the Navy's relevant surface and undersea needs and delivering results.  If it's on a surface combatant or submarine, chances are the Warfare Centers have touched that system in some form or fashion.  

We are experts in things like autonomy, lasers, additive manufacturing, energetics, mechanical and systems engineering, measurement science, sensors, explosive ordnance, littoral warfare, ship design and testing, and so much more.  Together we provide full-spectrum research, development, test & evaluation, engineering and fleet support for offensive and defensive systems associated with surface and undersea warfare. 
The advice and solutions our people develop and deploy across the entire life cycle of ship and submarine systems are not just ideas, they are technologies and solutions that fill capability gaps to reach our warfighting Sailors more quickly. 
 
Come join our forwarding-thinking WC teams who innovate as they go, quickly expanding the Navy's advantage by delivering real results. Come partner with the Navy Research & Development Establishment, Industry, Academia, and other government agencies in dynamic, collaborative professional exchanges. Whether it's in our world-class laboratories, one-of-a-kind facilities, or at sea aboard a surface combatant or submarine belonging to the world's most powerful navy, we need America's best and brightest to work with us. Come help deliver the decisive capabilities to keep America strong. Come deliver Science, Solutions, and Seapower to ships & Sailors.

#WarfightingExcellence
#ExecuteAndInnovate

Name: BİTES - Defence & Aerospace Technologies
Description:
Established in Istanbul in 2005, BİTES Defense & Aerospace Technologies is a national high-tech Defense Industry and Information Technologies company, currently continuing its activities in Ankara. In 2019, ASELSAN became a shareholder with 51% of the equity. With a strong and dynamic team consisting of nearly 500 people, BİTES keeps developing new technologies and solutions for various industries. 
The company’s primary focus is on next-generation technologies, such as Extended Reality and Image Processing, Artificial Intelligence, Modeling and Simulation Technologies, Avionic System Software, Safety-Critical Embedded Software and Cloud Technologies. 
BİTES ranked among “Fastest Growing Technology Companies of Turkey” and was granted “The Deloitte Fast 50 Award” for 3 consecutive years. In 2020, the company became one of the top 10 companies that makes the most R&D expenditure in Turkey in the Software and Information Technologies sector and ranked among the top 50 companies in all sectors. In 2023, BİTES became a subsidiary of ASELSAN by the acquisition of the remaining shares. 
For 20 years, our company has been offering high-quality solutions and services that create added value for its customers and business partners. Using the latest technology, our aim is to provide a competitive advantage to our partners and clients. 
Developing software-intensive systems and technologies with its highly qualified team, BİTES keeps working with determination and passion to create added value for our country, and aiming to offer its innovative products and solutions on a global scale.

Name: Savunma Sanayii Akademi
Description:

Cumhurbaşkanlığı Savunma Sanayii Başkanlığı bünyesinde kurulmuştur. Savunma Sanayii Akademi, savunma sanayii alanında yetişmiş nitelikli insan gücünün artırılmasına yönelik eğitim ve teknoloji programlarını geliştirmekte ve uygulamaktadır.


The Defense Industry Academy was established in 2017 within the Presidency of Defense Industries of the Republic of Turkey to contribute to the development of a qualified workforce needed by our country’s defense industry ecosystem. The Defense Industry Academy develops and implements training and technology programs aimed at increasing the number of qualified professionals in the defense industry.

Name: HENSOLDT South Africa
Description:
HENSOLDT South Africa brings together a comprehensive range of products, systems and services across defence and civil markets, from electronic warfare and optronics, to spectrum monitoring and security solutions.

On land, at sea or in the air, our world-class solutions detect and protect to make the world a safer place.

Name: Avibras
Description:
A Avibras é uma empresa brasileira de tecnologia e inovação com capacidade industrial única e reconhecida mundialmente pela excelência e qualidade de seus produtos, sistemas e soluções de engenharia nas áreas de Aeronáutica, Espaço, Eletrônica, Veículos e Defesa. 

Com mais de 60 anos de atuação e certificada como Empresa Estratégica de Defesa (EED), a Avibras consolidou sua posição como uma das empresas líderes mundiais no segmento de Defesa e Aeroespacial com o desenvolvimento e a fabricação de veículos especiais para fins militares, tornando-se uma referência em Sistema Lançador Múltiplo de Foguetes (MRLS), foguetes balísticos e guiados, mísseis e pioneira participação nos programas de pesquisa espacial.

Conhecida como uma empresa que tem “Competência para construir Competências”, a Avibras é uma System House, oferecendo soluções tecnológicas em atendimento a qualquer necessidade dos clientes, evidenciando a real diversificação e oportunidades de spin-off em Defesa, Espaço e Integração de Sistemas Complexos. 
Sediada em São José dos Campos, no Vale do Paraíba, a empresa é pioneira em tecnologia própria, inovadora e independente, e opera em amplas instalações em Jacareí e Lorena. Sua força de trabalho é composta por engenheiros, pesquisadores e técnicos altamente qualificados e capazes de prover soluções para os mais variados segmentos. 

Acesse o site da Avibras www.avibras.com.br e conheça as oportunidades de uma empresa, que reconhece talentos!

Name: Tonbo Imaging
Description:
Tonbo Imaging designs, builds and deploys advanced imaging and sensor systems to sense, understand and control complex environments. Tonbo Imaging offers a suite of solutions that address critical market needs in military reconnaissance, infrastructure security and transportation safety. The company’s offerings consist of sophisticated imaging products and intellectual property cores that can be licensed by OEMs and systems integrators.

The ability to see better and farther with the right contextual perspective for complete situational awareness is invaluable. Be it a soldier on the battlefield, an unmanned drone doing a reconnaissance mission in bad weather, a security guard looking through darkness and mist or a automotive driver navigating fog, all need the ability to sense the environment better, understand it in real-time and take control.

Tonbo Imaging leverages innovative designs in micro-optics, lower power electronics and real-time vision processing to design imaging systems for real world applications. Traditional systems across different imaging spectrums have relied on a single aperture principle with the burden of image formation placed excessively on the optics. Tonbo’s technology balances front end analog processing with sophisticated digital image processing to support the realization of light weight, low power imaging platforms across visible, mid wave IR and long wave IR spectrums.

TONBO is uniquely positioned in emerging markets. Tonbo offers state of the art electro optics technology without ITAR/Government restrictions. With vertically integrated design expertise in optics, electronics and computer vision and a large portfolio of intellectual property in imaging technologies, Tonbo is able to provide the best combination of technology and products at the best commercially competitive price

Name: Civils de la Défense
Description:
Rejoindre le ministère des Armées, c’est s’inscrire dans un projet professionnel clair, motivant et évolutif. C’est être acteur de son avenir professionnel.
De la cyberdéfense à la mécanique, des ressources humaines à la politique mémorielle, de la santé à l’environnement, avec ou sans diplômes et à tout niveau de responsabilités, le ministère des Armées propose 5 000 postes chaque année.
260 000 personnes - dont 63 200 agents civils - travaillent au côté des militaires dans les filières suivantes : technique, administrative, sociale, paramédicale.

Name: Nurol Teknoloji
Description:
Nurol Teknoloji, part of the Nurol Holding Group, was founded in 2008 to produce advanced ballistic ceramics. From the beginning, we have focused on research and development, achieving significant advancements, including developing high-temperature sinter furnaces for our production needs. Over the years, we have expanded from advanced technical ceramics to comprehensive ballistic solutions.

Our product range includes Alumina, Silicon Carbide, Boron Carbide ceramics, and Tungsten Carbide ceramics for civilian use. Based in Ankara, we operate two production facilities that manufacture high-tech ballistic ceramics and hybrid ballistic protective armor for friendly and allied countries worldwide, especially the Turkish Security Forces.

In 2023, we expanded our capabilities with the acquisition of Industrie Kreamike Hocherhein (IKH) in Germany, a leader in manufacturing advanced ceramic powder. Additionally, NT Armor and NT Cera, our U.S. branch, enhances our global presence and strengthens our commitment to delivering superior protection solutions.

As Türkiye's only advanced technical ceramic producer, we have positioned the country among the few globally capable of producing Boron Carbide ceramics with our unique processes. Our capabilities include a state-of-the-art machine park, skilled workforce, robust quality infrastructure, accredited ballistic labs, and a strong R&D vision. Our bespoke ballistic solutions for personnel, vehicle, and structural protection are trusted by security forces both domestically and internationally.

Committed to the responsibility of safeguarding lives, we strive daily to improve, providing our security forces with the most innovative, effective, lightweight, and reliable solutions against evolving threats.

Name: Defence Science and Technology Group (DSTG)
Description:
Defence Science and Technology Group (DSTG) is the Australian government's lead agency charged with applying science and technology to protect and defend Australia and its national interests. DSTG delivers expert, impartial advice and innovative solutions for the Department of Defence and other elements of national security.

DSTG’s vision is to be a world leader in defence science and technology – indispensable in transforming the Australian Defence Force and Australia’s national security.

DSTG seeds and shapes the perceptions and priorities of Defence for the use of science and technology and provides scientific and technical support to current Defence operations. Other key roles for DSTG include investigating future technologies for Defence and national security applications and ensuring Australia is a smart buyer and user of Defence equipment.

DSTG develops new Defence and national security capabilities, and enhances existing capabilities by increasing performance and safety and reducing the cost of ownership of Defence assets. We work collaboratively with other science and government agencies to strengthen national security and assist 
 industry to become better at supporting Defence's capability needs.

Headed by the Chief Defence Scientist, DSTG employs around 2,100 staff, predominantly scientists, engineers, IT specialists and technicians. 

DSTG has a presence in nearly every State and Territory in Australia.

Name: Havelsan Teknoloji Radar (HTR)
Description:
Havelsan Technology Radar (HTR) was established in 1990 with the aim of producing long-range early warning radars. It strengthened its operations in 2001 when HAVELSAN became its main shareholder.

The company carries out radar, electronic systems, network-supported security systems, card, cabling, unit system, assembly, and testing activities at its 35,500 m² facility.

HTR, which was awarded the title of Ministry-approved R&D (Research and Development) Center in 2017, continues to grow by designing and manufacturing original and national products thanks to investments made with its own resources.

HTR continues to move forward confidently into the future with its innovative technologies and national solutions.

Name: F-35 Joint Program Office
Description: The F-35 Lightning II Program is the Department of Defense's focal point for defining affordable next generation strike aircraft weapon systems for the Navy, Air Force, Marines, and our allies. The F-35 brings cutting-edge technologies to the battlespace of the future.

Name: BAE Systems Saudi Arabia
Description:
BAE Systems is one of the world’s largest aerospace and defense companies provide leading services in engineering, technology and security. 7,300 of our employees work in Saudi Arabia and 74% of them are Saudis, making us one of the world’s leading private sector employers of Saudis.

We work together with local partners to develop, engineer, manufacture and support the innovations that sustain economic growth, increase defense sovereignty and safeguard commercial interests. This is supported by our strong relationships with local industrial partners, and underlined by our nearly 50 years of heritage in providing leading edge technology and training to the Saudi Armed Forces.

the Company has contributed to the training of thousands of Saudi Nationals in many highly-skilled fields, including engineering, technology and aviation. In addition to this, BAE Systems Saudi Arabia has provided a range of solutions to many areas of the military, including electronics, security systems and information technology.

We continued to build upon our commitment to Saudi economic growth and the development of a local aerospace and defense industry, & we are proud taking  significant steps towards the transfer of capability, skills and knowledge to the people and businesses of the Kingdom. 

As part of this commitment, BAE Systems Saudi Arabia has invested in many Saudi companies which it has developed to provide leading services, support and expertise in the fields of aviation, technology and security. These companies are Advanced Electronics Company, International Systems Engineering and Saudi Development and Training and Saudi Maintenance and Supply Chain Management Company.

Name: IVECO DEFENCE VEHICLES
Description: Iveco Defence Vehicles is a company making part of Iveco Group N.V. Iveco Defence Vehicles is dedicated to delivering innovative automotive and protection solutions to meet the needs of military customers worldwide. The company manufactures specialist logistic, protected and armoured vehicles in its facility in Bolzano in Northern Italy, as well as marketing Iveco’s full commercial range, adapted as necessary to meet the demands of the military user. In consequence, Iveco Defence Vehicles has a full range of vehicles to meet a broad spectrum of defence applications.

Name: ANOVA Ar-Ge Teknolojileri
Description:
ANOVA Ar-Ge Teknolojileri, mekanik, elektronik ve gömülü yazılım tasarımı, analiz/simülasyon, üretim, test ve entegrasyon yetkinliklerine sahip, %100 yerli sermayeli bir teknoloji şirketidir. Havacılık ve savunma sanayii için çevresel kontrol sistemleri, yakıt sistemleri, silah sistemleri ve güç elektroniği gibi kritik alt sistemleri yerli, milli ve özgün olarak tasarlayan ANOVA, grup şirketleriyle birlikte 400'ü aşkın yetkin personeliyle faaliyet göstermektedir. Şirket, anahtar teslim çözümler sunmanın yanı sıra, fikri mülkiyet haklarına sahip olduğu mühendislik katma değeri yüksek ürünlerle yurt içi ve yurt dışındaki müşterilerine hizmet vermektedir. Özgün ve yenilikçi sistemler geliştirerek global bir oyuncu olma vizyonuyla hareket eden ANOVA, bugüne kadar 15 farklı ülkeye ihracat gerçekleştirmiştir.
Grup bünyesinde yer alan Harpax, Numesys, Atlas Global Mekatronik ve Geom iştirakleriyle savunma ve havacılık sektörüne yönelik mekanik, termal, elektronik tasarım, simülasyon, yazılım geliştirme, üretim, test ve kalifikasyon süreçlerini uluslararası standartlarda yürütmektedir.

Name: Tawazun Council for Defence Enablement
Description:
Tawazun Council for Defence Enablement is an independent government entity that works closely with the Ministry of Defence and security agencies in the UAE. It aims to enhance the capabilities of the defence and security sector through developing innovative solutions that support the sustainability of the military and security industries.

The Council works on balancing between the needs of the local sector and defense requirements, with a focus on improving operational quality and efficiency. The Council also seeks to develop a regulatory environment that ensures the sustainability of technological innovation, enhancing national security and attracting various investments in this field. 

Through this, the Council contributes to empowering national capabilities, promoting local content, and accelerating growth in the sector by supporting acquisition and development strategies.

Name: Patrouille de France
Description:
Sous la cocarde bleu, blanc, rouge, la Patrouille de France, appelée aussi PAF (Patrouille Acrobatique de France) est une unité des équipes de présentation de l’Armée de l’Air et de l'Espace. Sa mission est d’être une des ambassadrices de l’Armée de l’Air et de l'Espace en France comme à l’étranger et de représenter tous les aviateurs qui oeuvrent au quotidien dans notre institution. 

À ce titre, la mission de représentation impose à ces ambassadeurs une disponibilité, une rigueur et un investissement important, aussi bien en vol qu’au sol avec un rythme différent entre la saison hivernale (entraînements) et la saison estivale (manifestations aériennes). Ses 9 pilotes et 39 mécaniciens, tous issus des escadrons de combat de l’Armée de l’Air et de l'Espace, ont vocation à y retourner à l’issue de leur passage à la Patrouille de France.

Considérée comme l’une des plus prestigieuses formations acrobatiques au monde, la Grande Dame évolue dans le ciel avec grâce et élégance depuis 70 ans. Elle ouvre traditionnellement le défilé du 14 Juillet à Paris, mais réalise avant tout des démonstrations aériennes à destination du plus grand nombre, conçues comme un spectacle aérien.

Name: Defence Procurement
Description:
The Ministry of Defence (MOD) is one of the biggest public procurement organisations in Europe and the UK industry’s largest single customer, managing some of the most complex and technologically advanced requirements in the world. 

MOD Commercial & Industry are part of the National Armaments Director Group: https://www.gov.uk/government/groups/national-armaments-director-nad-group. We advertise all our MOD procurement opportunities over the value of £10k on the Defence Sourcing Portal. You can register and view all the UK MOD opportunities free of charge. We work closely with a number of major suppliers to ensure delivery of the defence programme on large and complex projects. Around 75% of our procurement opportunities exist within our supply chain. 

We want to attract new and non-traditional suppliers into Defence and also promote access to funding and support for innovation through our communication channels. Follow us here on LinkedIn for information including webinars, announcements and opportunities. 

For more information on how to find opportunities in the defence supply chain, please visit our GOV.UK page: https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/ministry-of-defence/about/procurement 

Defence Sourcing Portal: https://www.contracts.mod.uk/web/login.html

Name: Babcock Australia & New Zealand
Description:
Be part of something BIG!

For over 130 years, Babcock has been a trusted guardian of national security, protecting lives and maintaining lines of defence across the globe.

From maritime sustainment and secure communications, to lifesaving helicopter missions and managing critical fleets, we ensure our customers in defence and civil markets across Australasia are equipped to fulfil their mission.

Whether you’re just starting out, looking to apply your existing skills to a different challenge or interested in advancing your career to the next level, you can be part of a worldwide defence and aerospace company.

Our people are our core. We are collaborative, committed, and work at pace to get the job done. Each one of us plays our part in supporting our local communities and our nation’s security.

Join us in creating a safe and secure world, together.

Name: Dynetics, Inc.
Description: Leidos Dynetics provides responsive, cost-effective engineering, scientific, IT solutions to the national security, cybersecurity, space, and critical infrastructure sectors. Our portfolio features highly specialized technical services and a range of software and hardware products, including components, subsystems, and complex end-to-end systems.

Name: EHSİM Elektronik Harp Sistemleri
Description:
EHSİM, elektronik harp sistemleri alanında faaliyet göstermek üzere 1998 yılında kurulmuştur. Ülkemizin öncü savunma sanayii şirketlerinin iştiraki olan EHSİM’in hisselerinin %50’si ASELSAN’a, %49’u HAVELSAN’a ve %1’i SSTEK’e aittir. 

Elektronik harp ve pasif radar sistemleri tasarlayan, geliştiren, üreten; entegre lojistik destek faaliyetlerini yürüten; yüksek teknolojili ürün yelpazesine sahip olan bir savunma sanayii kuruluşu olan EHSİM, küresel ölçekte, sektörün öncü şirketlerinden biri olmayı hedeflemekte; vizyoner ve dinamik uzman kadrosu ile hedeflerine yürüyüşünü kararlı adımlarla sürdürmektedir.

Name: Defence Connect
Description:
Defence Connect (www.defenceconnect.com.au) is a media and market intelligence platform focused on increasing the connectivity of Australia’s defence industry and helping the sector capitalise on the largest spend in defence capability building since the Second World War.

It received the highly coveted accolade of Business Website of the Year at the 2018 Mumbrella Publish Awards.

The Defence Connect brand has gained rapid engagement with Defence and the defence industry and is considered an essential source for breaking news and market intelligence for the sector.

The platform now connects with 100,000+ defence professionals every month, delivering essential information to help organisations make more informed business decisions and deliver greater defence capabilities.

Defence Connect engages senior leadership across the defence industry including CASG, DOD, DST Group, Navy, Army, Airforce, Intelligence and Joint Operations Command, Federal and State Ministries and related departments, prime contractors, SMEs and Defence Force establishments, institutes, academics and think tanks.

Defence Connect leverages digital distribution channels including web, podcast, social, research, broadcast and direct communications to support defence business.

Disclaimer:
Neither the Australian Defence Industry Awards nor Defence Connect are sponsored, approved by, affiliated with or endorsed in any way by the Commonwealth of Australia or the Department of Defence.

Momentum Media – the business behind Defence Connect – has been highly awarded for its ability to create energised and highly-engaged professional communities. It connects over a million professionals across Australia’s finance, space and defence, real estate, legal, accounting, wealth, funds management, retirement, mortgages and SME sectors. 

SUBSCRIBE TODAY
For breaking news and market intelligence delivered each day subscribe to Defence Connect on https://www.defenceconnect.com.au/subscribe

Name: Throttle Aerospace Systems Pvt Ltd
Description:
Throttle Aerospace Systems Pvt. Ltd (TAS). an India’s fastest growing drone manufacturer located in Bangalore, India, formed by a team of aviation geeks having 15+ years of professional experience in Design, Engineering, manufacturing, software development & supply chain carried from Aerospace and Defence Industries globally.

Throttle Aerospace Systems (TAS) is part of NeoSky and RattanIndia Enterprises (REL). RattanIndia group (‘the Group’) is a business conglomerate with business interest across thermal power production & distribution, eCommerce, Drones, EV’s, FinTech and Fashion brands.. The Group has two listed companies, having a combined revenue of around Rs.7300 Cr (~$900 Mn) in 2022, a market cap of around Rs.8000 Cr (~ $1 Bn) and employs around 2700 employees. NeoSky is a full-line drone player with presence into Drone Product , Service and Training. TAS has India’s first DGCA-certified drone, having license to make drones for MoD (Ministry of Defence), DGCA-Type Certification and also qualified for Government of India’s PLI (Production Linked Incentive) scheme. 

TAS is India’s first company to complete BVLOS trials and submit recommendations to DGCA. We have a range of Drones for Land mapping, Surveillance, Cargo, Inspection, Agriculture, Disaster management and many more. Our product portfolio includes DOPO, TACT, Nimble, L-Series, Defender and more. TAS’s parent company, NeoSky runs a DGCA approved, drone RPTO (Remote Pilot Training School) and also offers a range of Drone services.

Name: Paras Defence & Space Technologies Ltd.
Description:
Paras Defence and Space Technologies Ltd. is focused on the Design, Development, Manufacturing, Testing & Commissioning of Products, Systems and Solutions having Defence & Space Applications. We strongly follow the principle of Make in India and aim at being a true “IDDM” Company offering various products and solutions that are being manufactured under one roof. We have a start-of-the-art manufacturing facility and a highly skilled work force along with being equipped with a robust supply chain group.

We have the capability to handle turnkey projects of all sizes and have the resource scalability required to handle mega projects.

Our Expertise: 
-	Ultra - Precision Optics & Optical Assemblies for Defence & Space Applications. 
-	IR Lenses, Multifold Lenses, Lens Barrel, Space Deployable Antennas. 
-	MIL Grade High Performance Embedded Electronic Systems, Rugged Displays, Computing Systems, Command & Control Consoles.
-	Turnkey & Indigenous EMP Protection Solutions & Installation by our Team for Strategic Locations. 
-	Automated Test Equipment, Defence Automation Solutions, MIL Grade COTS Products.
-	Heavy Engineering Solutions for Critical Defence Projects. 
-	Flow Formed Tubes, Military Winches, Rack Mount Systems.

Contact Us at: business@parasdefence.com

Name: Barrett Firearms Mfg, Inc
Description:
Barrett is the world leader in large-caliber rifle system design and manufacturing. Our products are used by civilian sport shooters, law enforcement agencies, the United States military and more than 80 American allied countries across the world.

And we continue to grow. In fact, we recently moved into a new state-of-the-art corporate headquarters, allowing us to expand our research and development, manufacturing, quality control and shipping capabilities.

No matter how successful we become, however, our story keeps evolving. Because, at Barrett, we don’t believe in resting on our laurels. In fact, we take great pride in thinking outside the box and exploring ways to continue to improve. After all, if there’s one thing our success has taught us, it’s this: You’re only as good as your last accomplishment. 

Whether we’re carefully assembling our latest rifle, engineering scope rings that are stronger than ever or increasing the velocity and precision of the ammunition itself — we’re always working with one goal in mind: absolute perfection.

Description: The JAIC is an AI fielding activity for the Department of Defense

Name: Cyber Jobs Hunting
Description: A place for anyone in security to come find work and network with peers

Name: Engility Corporation
Description: Engility Corporation is now part of SAIC, a premier technology integrator based in Reston, Virginia. For more information about SAIC, visit www.linkedin.com/company/SAICinc.

Name: International Astronautical Federation
Description:
International Astronautical Federation
'Connecting @ll Space People'

The IAF was created in 1951 with the aim of advancing knowledge about space, and developing the application of space assets for the benefit of humanity. Today the IAF continues to foster dialogue between scientists, academics and the space industry around the world, and support international cooperation in all space-related activities.

The Federation is the world’s leading space advocacy body with over 513 members, including space agencies and offices, industry, professional societies, associations, universities, museums and R&D organisations across 77 countries.

More than 30 technical committees and 17 administrative committees support the Federation in its mission to advance knowledge about space, and to foster the development of space assets by facilitating global cooperation.

Name: Infodefensa
Description: Infodefensa, medio online especializado en información de seguridad y defensa de referencia en español, proporciona información de forma diaria, independiente y útil para el sector, ofreciendo un conocimiento global sobre nuevas tecnologías, tendencias y empresas de la industria de la Defensa y Seguridad en los mercados de España y América. Infodefensa es el primer medio especializado en defensa con presencia en las redes sociales más importantes como LinkedIn, Twitter, Facebook y YouTube.

Name: Aeronautics Group
Description:
Aeronautics Group is a world leading developer and manufacturer of defense solutions based on Unmanned Aerial Systems and advanced ISTAR systems. Aeronautics’ UAS line of products enable the military, homeland security and law enforcement customers to ensure the safety of millions of people.

 Aeronautics Group in-house vertical integration capabilities facilitate rapid delivery of tailored turnkey solutions to its customers. Since its establishment in 1997, the Company's products have been delivered and successfully deployed by over 70 defense, military and homeland security forces on five continents. Aeronautics Group offers a ‘one-stop shop’ for cost-effective solutions for defense, HLS and border protection missions.

Aeronautics fundamental goal is to create the right partnership and cooperation with customers and local industries worldwide, in order to build mutual capabilities of technologies and innovation for the benefit of our partners and customers.

Name: IDS International
Description:
IDS International is a global leader in providing comprehensive Training & Technical Services for international government projects. Our core services encompass security provision for government-owned construction sites, escorting visitors through secure facilities, and delivering extensive military training. This includes organization, battlefield communication, driving military vehicles, basic procedures, and advanced operations such as missile operations.

Our Infrastructure Protection division is dedicated to safeguarding mission-critical infrastructure and facilities, even in the most hostile and challenging operational environments. We are experienced in operating in conflict zones, delivering innovative solutions for 21st-century problems.

Our Expeditionary Support team provides life support services, procurement and logistics, engineering, program management, facilities operations and maintenance, construction, workforce training, deployment, and oversight. We also offer Specialized Training services to ensure that warfighters are equipped with the skills needed to navigate ever-changing global geo-political environments.

In the realm of Cyber & Information Warfare, our team of leading experts and field practitioners is experienced in conflict, development, stabilization, the cyber domain, and human factors that influence operating environments. We work closely with our clients to gain an in-depth understanding of their objectives and challenges, allowing us to deliver quality, multidisciplinary solutions to even the most complex problems.

With nearly 70 years of combined experience, we bring a wealth of knowledge and expertise to our clients. Our innovative approach and operational expertise allow us to deliver customized solutions to meet the unique needs of companies in business development, union military contingents, defense, diplomacy, development, guard services, security detail, and government, military, nonprofit, and public-sector clients.

Name: Orbit Fab
Description: Orbit Fab envisions a thriving in-space market for products and services that support both existing space businesses (communications and Earth observation) and new industries like space tourism, manufacturing, and mining. The first step is achieving ubiquitous availability of satellite propellant in Earth orbit, expanding the operational potential of new and existing space assets and providing unprecedented business model flexibility for satellite owners. The future for satellites is no longer restricted to the fuel they are launched with. It is about getting the fuel and other materials they need, where and when they need it, to accomplish things never before thought possible.

Name: Commissariat des armées
Description:
Le service du commissariat des armées (SCA) est en charge du soutien et de l’administration générale pour le ministère et les armées en particulier, en métropole, outre-mer, à l’étranger et en opérations extérieures (OPEX). Son action s’inscrit dans une démarche de « bout en bout », de la définition d’une politique de soutien avec l’expertise nécessaire jusqu’à la production et la délivrance, dans chacune des onze fonctions dédiées. Il met en œuvre à ce titre l’ensemble des politiques ministérielles qui relèvent de ses domaines de compétence.
Responsable des achats pour la vie courante, du transport des forces, de l’exécution des dépenses relevant du soutien commun au profit des armées, le SCA est également compétent dans les différents domaines du soutien de l’homme tels que les équipements individuels (habillement) et collectifs (matériels de campagne), vivres opérationnels (rations de combat et alimentation embarquée), du transport de personnel et de la gestion des véhicules de la gamme commerciale du ministère. Il est aussi en charge de la fonction restauration ainsi que de la gestion base vie comprenant entre autre l’hébergement et l’hôtellerie. 
Enfin, il assure une fonction mutualisée d’administration du personnel, exécute le service de la solde des militaires, gère les frais de déplacement et les changements de résidence du ministère. Il exerce également une fonction de conseil juridique au commandement. 
Pour réaliser cette mission, il s’appuie sur une organisation ancrée dans les territoires et sur une plasticité native lui permettant des bascules d’effort entre l’ensemble de ses composantes. Sa performance s’exprime par la recherche permanente de la meilleure satisfaction des besoins sur le périmètre soutenu alliée à l’efficience dans l’emploi des ressources et la résilience opérationnelle de ses processus et de son personnel. 
Le directeur central du service du commissariat des armées (SCA) et le commissaire général hors classe Olivier Marcotte.

Name: CAE USA
Description:
CAE USA, located in Tampa Florida, provides modeling and simulation technologies, integrated training solutions and training system support services for various defense and government agencies in the USA, Central and South America. CAE USA provides blended training solutions, fully integrated simulation products, and training services and curriculum that are capable of meeting military customers and original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) training requirements.

CAE's Defense and Security business unit focuses on helping prepare military customers to develop and maintain the highest levels of mission readiness. CAE USA, located in Tampa Florida, is part of CAE's Defense and Security business unit with specific responsibility for serving the United States, South America and select international markets. CAE USA includes over 1,000 employees and is the largest segment within CAE's Defense and Security business unit. The organization leverages the full breadth of CAE's capabilities, technologies and solutions and provides simulation products, training services and training centers that contribute to the preparedness and readiness of United States and allied warfighters. 

CAE USA is a U.S. corporation that operates under a Special Security Agreement (SSA) with the U.S. Department of Defense. As a U.S. corporation operating under an SSA, CAE USA has its own U.S. board of directors that monitors and oversees all company activities related to classified and export-controlled information, security procedures, and company management. The President and General Manager of CAE USA reports directly to the CAE USA Board of Directors.

Vinnell Arabia is an international corporation based in Saudi Arabia dedicated to providing the best in military training, logistics and support to the Ministry of the National Guard (MNG) in the form of organization development, weapon systems maintenance and management consultancy.

Name: U.S. Army Security Assistance Command
Description:
USASAC, which is headquartered at Redstone Arsenal, leads the AMC Security Assistance Enterprise; develops and manages security assistance programs and foreign military sales cases to build partner capacity, support COCOM engagement strategies, and strengthen U.S. global partnerships.  Dr. Myra Gray, SES, Deputy to the Commanding General of the U.S. Army Security Assistance Command (USASAC), will provide the senior leader perspective on foreign military sales and security assistance across the globe.

Total Package
To carry out the Army security assistance mission, USASAC relies on Army Materiel Command Life Cycle Management Commands as well as other Department of Defense agencies and U.S. industry to support its processes. Each sale of equipment to overseas customers comprises the same “total package” of quality materiel, facilities, spare parts, training, publications, technical documentation, sustainment and other services that AMC provides to U.S. Army units.

Training
The U.S. Army Security Assistance Training Management Organization, a subordinate command of USASAC, facilitates deployment of training teams throughout the world to provide training tailored to a country for equipment purchased through FMS. The program consistently has teams deployed worldwide.

Benefits of Security Assistance: 
• One of the principal elements of the U.S. Foreign Policy
• Regulated by: Foreign Assistance Act and Arms Export Control Act
• Executed by the Department of Defense under Department of State oversight
• Designed to promote regional stability
• Supports COCOM goals and objectives

Worldwide Impact of FMS: 
• Enhances Political-Military Ties
• Provides for military compatibility and interoperability
• Enhances defense industry interest of both nations
• Reduces production costs
• U.S. Army (and DoD) are strong advocates for U.S. industry
• Keeps industry production lines “hot”

Desde então, tem oferecido soluções eficientes e ferramentas de suporte a decisões para os mais diversos setores. É reconhecida pela excelência de seus produtos e serviços em todo o Brasil. Name: NDIA Special Operations Division
Description:
NDIA’s Special Operations Division mission is to help industry, government, and the public understand the critical role of special operations (SO) and low-intensity conflict (LIC) forces in US national security strategy and how these forces are integrated into the military structure.

A big part of that work is facilitating cooperation between industry and SO and LIC forces to assure that those forces have access to ideas and technology from the U.S. industrial and academic base. The division gives voice to the concerns of the SO/LIC community and champions new, innovative ideas to meet the national security challenges of the 21st century. The division is composed of subject matter experts from the defense industry who, aided by government liaison participants, work to resolve difficult issues facing SO and LIC forces.

Through meetings and symposia, the division provides information on a variety of matters involving special operations, low-intensity conflict and related national security matters, including counternarcotics, combating terrorism, consequence management resulting from terrorist events, civil affairs, psychological operations, force protection, humanitarian operations and peacekeeping. The functional purview of the division follows the roles and missions of the assistant secretary of defense for SO/LIC, and the U.S. Special Operations Command commander. Division activities provide a forum for discussion of SO and LIC challenges and requirements and provide an opportunity for SO and LIC leaders to present the policy, concepts, and procedures guiding their respective commands and agencies in meeting national security goals.

The division’s highlight event is the annual SO/LIC Symposium and Award Dinner.  During the Awards Dinner, the division presents various awards to individuals who have made lasting contributions, singular or lifetime, to the SO/LIC community.

Name: Arms Corporation of the Philippines
Description:
In 1905, two Englishmen named Roy Squires and William Bingham put up a photo print shop and merchandising firm in the Philippines. The company established was called Squires, Bingham & Co. Squires and Bingham eventually expanded their business to sporting goods - including firearms and ammunition.

In 1941, the store - referred to as the "Sportsmen's Headquarters" - found a new owner in Celso S. Tuason. Unfortunately, just four months after he purchased the store, World War II broke out and Japan invaded the Philippines!

After the war, due to foreign exchange controls, Don Celso Tuason decided to venture into manufacturing. He focused on the most popular and profitable item in the store - firearms and ammunition. In 1952, the President of the Philippines granted the Tuason family permission to manufacture firearms and ammunition locally as it was in line with his governments thrust towards industrialization. This led to the birth of Squires Bingham Manufacturing. Inc.

In the mid-1960's, Don Celson Tuason decided that it was time to turn over the management of the Company to his three sons - Bolo, Butch and Conkoy. In 1980, Squires Bingham Co., Inc. became the holding company of the Celso S. Tuason family and their varied business interests. Don Celso's eldest son, Demetrio "Bolo" Tuason, was chosen to be the Chairman and President of the manufacturing company for firearms and ammunition. Thus, Arms Corporation of the Philippines (Armscor) was born.

Name: German Institute for Defence and Strategic Studies
Description:
Die Welt ist im Umbruch und die Rahmenbedingungen für Sicherheitspolitik verändern sich rasant. Dies stellt die Bundeswehr wie auch die Bundesregierung vor neue strategische Herausforderungen. Um fundierte, wissenschaftlich basierte Antworten auf diese Herausforderungen geben zu können, wurde im Sommer 2018 das German Institute for Defence and Strategic Studies (GIDS) gegründet. Es ist eine Kooperation der Führungsakademie der Bundeswehr und der Helmut-Schmidt-Universität / Universität der Bundeswehr Hamburg.

Das GIDS untersucht und erforscht strategische Fragestellungen aus sicherheits- und verteidigungspolitischer Perspektive. Damit schafft es eine Basis für die Beratung der Entscheidungsträger in Bundeswehr und Bundesregierung. Das GIDS konzentriert sich auf Forschungsfragen, die derzeit bei keinem anderen sicherheitspolitischen Thinktank im Zentrum des Interesses stehen. Dabei kann das Institut auf die herausragende Expertise beider Bildungseinrichtungen, Universität und Führungsakademie, zurückgreifen. Dies erlaubt interdisziplinäre und in ihrer methodischen Breite einzigartige Forschung und aus dieser resultierend bedarfsorientierte Beratung. Das GIDS verfügt zudem über ein wachsendes internationales wissenschaftliches und militärfachliches Netzwerk. Zugleich baut das GIDS ein systematisiertes Wissensmanagement auf. Das Ziel: Die an Akademie und Universität entstandenen Studien, Abschluss- und Qualifizierungsarbeiten sowie weitere Ergebnisse aus Forschung und Lehre sollen für die Wissenschaft und Öffentlichkeit zugänglich gemacht werden.

Name: C4i Systems
Description:
C4i Systems designs, produces and integrates cutting edge Command, Control, Computers, Communications, Intelligence, Surveillance, Target Acquisition and Reconnaissance (C4ISTAR) Technologies. 

From mission critical systems to complete infrastructure transformation, our bespoke solutions and customised products help our clients in the defence, security and commercial sectors worldwide to harness the benefits these technologies can offer.

The nature of our clients’ responsibilities means they need the right solution at the right price at the right time. Our agility, flexibility, integrity, innovation and professionalism are key to ensuring that we always deliver against these demanding requirements.

We recognise that our success is built on the qualities of our people. Our engineers have backgrounds in the defence and security sectors which means they have first-hand experience of how operational environments work and believe in setting and achieving the most exacting standards. They also have the extensive experience of working with some of the leading industrial organisations in the world such as Harris, Lockheed Martin, Thales, Fujitsu, General Dynamics and Honeywell, so they understand and enforce technological and operational excellence in everything they do.

Name: Demmer Corporation
Description:
The mission of Demmer Corporation is to create a competitive advantage for our customers by creating and executing world class engineering and manufacturing solutions. Our flawless launch focus and rigorous process improvement environment drives our ability to acquire new business opportunities and grow customer relationships.

"We are a company that's based on integrity. Where we make promises, we keep our promises and we work very hard to see how we can help a customer enhance their position in their market." - Bill Demmer, President and CEO

Today, Demmer Corporation proudly serves defense, aerospace, automotive, energy, and transportation customers through multiple plants across the mid Michigan region. Demmer's employees are responsible for daily manufacturing operations in 1,600,000 square feet of available manufacturing floor space.

Name: Compass, Inc.
Description:
Compass, Inc. (Compass) is a Small Business headquartered in Winchester, VA as a Defense and Intelligence solutions provider to the United States Government. We provide Systems Engineering and Technical Assistance (SETA), Advisory and Assistance Services (A&AS), and Systems Engineering and Integration (SE&I) to our government and business partner customers. As a premier Defense and Intelligence solution provider, we employee a diverse, agile, highly trained and extremely talented staff.  

Compass wants to assure you that you have the resources available to help you both personally and professionally when you become a member of our team. One way we accomplish this is by offering you a high quality medical, dental, life insurance, 401(k) savings plan and disability benefits package. In addition, you are eligible for company-paid vacations, holidays, sick leave, and educational assistance to round out Compass's generous package.  
Compass operates as a Prime and Subcontractor to Industry Partners supporting a variety of customers ranging across: Office of the Secretary of Defense (OSD) staff; Combatant Commands (CCMDs); Subordinate Joint Commands; Military Service Commands/Installations; Intelligence Agencies; and Acquisition Program Management Offices (PMOs). 

Compass operational locations: Winchester, VA ; Huntsville, AL , Omaha, NE; and on-site at customer locations nationwide.

Name: J3Seven
Description:
Real-world experience

J3Seven Pty Ltd was incorporated in Australia on 12 July 2012. Operated by former Australian Defence Force personnel, we are Australia’s leading CBRNE and exploitation experts. With over 65 years of real-world field experience, we have a deep understanding of the operator, equipment, and environmental interfaces. This means we are perfectly positioned to provide Australia’s first responders, military forces, border control and other security agencies with tomorrow’s cutting-edge solutions to meet the demands of today’s challenges. 

World-leading capabilities 

J3Seven is the sole Australian distributor and accredited servicing agent for many of the most respected and well-known CBRNE and exploitation brands. We specialise in bringing world’s leading equipment manufacturers and our customer’s needs together. Through strong partnerships we actively work on our customer’s behalf to research, develop, source and deliver world-class products, training, and maintenance solutions.

360º solutions

Our careful analysis and unique insight allows us to develop and deliver world-class capabilities tailored to customer needs. Our 360º through-life solutions are driven by customer satisfaction and continuous improvement encompassing quality management, and expert project management and delivery. This ensures the best solutions are delivered, trained and supported in accordance with our high standards.

Veteran-owned business

As a 100% Australian veteran-owned business, J3Seven provides quality Australian Industry Capability. Together, we are changing how the Australian CBRNE and exploitation market is perceived around the world. Stable and secure, we are small enough to be agile but big enough to successfully deliver world-class capability.

Name: Rotair Aerospace Corporation
Description: For over fifty years, Rotair Aerospace has been a producer of spares for various Rotary Wing Aircraft including Sikorsky, Bell, and Boeing. For Sikorsky Helicopters, we continue to support all the spares for the Sikorsky S-61 Sea King, UH-60 Blackhawk, and CH-53 Platforms.  Our Aerospace Quality Management System is registered for Standards under current ISO 9001 and AS9100 Certifications.

Name: NINE 30 CONSULTING
Description:
Nine 30 Consulting is a small business located in Lexington Park, MD that provides financial management, project management, acquisition, and data analytics support to various organizations within the Naval Air Warfare Center, Aircraft Division (NAWCAD) and Naval Air Systems  Command (NAVAIR). We are a numbers company and the most important number in government financial management is NINE 30, or September 30th, the last day of the government fiscal year which drives vital tactical and strategic decisions.

NINE 30 CONSULTING strives to deliver premiere analytical services to the federal government in the disciplines of financial, project, and risk management while offering unparalleled career opportunities, experiences, and benefits to our employees.

We are actively recruiting for positions supporting missions at the Naval Air Warfare Center, Aircraft Division (NAWCAD) at Saint Inigoes, MD and the Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) at Patuxent River, MD.  Please go to https://www.nine30consulting.com/careers.html for details on each position and to apply.

Name: Military Systems and Technology
Description:
Military Systems and Technology has established itself as a comprehensive resource of information and news from international defence companies, associations and events.

We established Military Systems and Technology in 2006 as an online portal for defence companies to promote their capabilities to defence procurement organisations and other defence companies around the world.

Since those early days we now have over 300 companies present on the website and the number continues to grow.  Over the last five years the average number of visitors to the site has also continued to grow year on year with over 250,000 in 2019.

Each company entry consists of an introduction, product or service description, news releases, brochures, videos and images enabling visitors to the site to gain a clear understanding of the company, the entry also provides full contact details.

Our users come from across all parts of the globe and includes Ministries/Departments of Defence, Procurement Agencies, Heads of Armed Forces to the soldier, sailor or airman who will be the ultimate users, together with engineers and purchasers across industry.

We have recently updated our web portal to give you a new and better experience able to find information even more quickly. This update also ensures your company’s products and services are on the front page of search engine results.

We publish an online magazine which gives companies, who provide content on the website, an opportunity to promote their capabilities to a wide global marketplace.

Military Systems and Technology has taken its mission of helping companies to increase their sales one step further with the introduction of the Defence Design Agency.  The DDA team works with defence and aerospace clients to help them with exhibition design, brochures, developing websites, adverts and editorial material – a real partnership approach to help you get the most “bang for your buck”.

Name: H2 Analytics

H2 Analytics supports our clients with full service solutions to drive their decision making processes. 

As career intelligence and defence professionals, we have spent our careers helping decision makers turn information into intelligence, and intelligence into decisions. 

From intelligence support and consulting services, to software development and integration, H2 Analytics can support your organization in turning information into decisions.

Name: Acquisition Instructor Course
Description:
AQIC is the DAF’s premier training ground for informed, connected acquirers who are part of a larger network reshaping the way the Acq community interacts with & supports the warfighter. 

AQIC is a highly selective, 5.5-month course for mid-level professionals in the Program Management, Engineering, Contracting & Financial Management fields. It is the Acq community's equivalent to the USAF Weapons School. 

The VCSAF & VCSO called upon AQIC to create an avenue for constant operator & acquirer collaboration to fulfill DAF strategic goals, accelerate the kill chain & maintain our competitive advantage - both in air & space. 

We produce graduates who embody 6 key attributes: Humble, Approachable, Credible, Creative, Collaborative and Influential. Grads make use of their cross-functional knowledge & extensive networks to bridge the gap between the Acq, Ops & Industry communities. Initiating this at the tactical level opens up a world of both formal & informal comm lines that allows us to exchange info & be more effective in the process of innovating solutions to maintain warfighter advantage.

Name: RAF Cosford Air Show
Description:
RAF Cosford Air Show 2023 - Sunday 11th June 2023

The RAF Cosford Air Show is held annually at RAF Cosford in Shropshire. It is the largest one day event in the West Midlands regularly welcoming over 50,000 visitors.  The Air Show is a fantastic opportunity for RAF personnel to engage with the public,showcasing their skills and providing a stage for recruitment support. 


Please contact the Air Show Office if you would like to find out more about sponsorship  and hospitality opportunities, being involved in our STEM Hangar, Trade & Exhibition stands or bringing a coach excursion along to the Air Show.

Name: The Tech7 Company
Description: Founded in 2015 and built on a reputation for honesty, innovation, and quality – Tech7 is a world class provider of rapid solutions development, complex systems integration, and the onboarding of commercial capabilities.  Our team of experts has vast experience in complex systems gained from working across various sectors. With this experience, we offer end-to-end support throughout the integration of new technologies, from initial concept to ongoing operations and maintenance. TECH7 is an Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) and an 8(a) Small Disadvantaged Business (SDB).

Name: Blücher GmbH
Description:
BLÜCHER is the leading company in the development and production of adsorptive compound materials for the protection against chemical and biological warfare agents. With over 50 years of experience, BLÜCHER stands for advanced engineering sciences, analytical solutions and on-demand protective technologies and applications.

In accordance with our tradition of innovative excellence, we control the entire value chain in the manufacture of sorptive textile high-performance composites – from raw materials to complex system solutions by the company’s own development and production. 

SARATOGA’s tailor-made solutions for protection against CB threats have made Blücher a world market leader in this field. SARATOGA CB protective equipment provides mission-oriented protection and innovative solutions for specific requirements in the fields of military and civil defense as well as for fire brigades and the police. The latest products are the results of ongoing developments. Lighter and stretchable materials broaden the range of protection integrating aerosol protection, reducing burden and increasing operational effectiveness. 

Globally, the SARATOGA technology is used by more than 53 nations, including 27 NATO member states. To date, over 12 million SARATOGA protective suits have been delivered to customers worldwide.

Since its foundation by Hasso and Hubert von Blücher in 1969, the Blücher Group has maintained its high ethical and technological standards. The protection of life is central to all our activities – we protect those who protect us!

Name: Defence Seals & Spares
Description:
Defence Seals & Spares is Australia’s leading supplier of military-grade components required for both new build acquisition and through-life sustainment of defence platforms and systems.

We are also manufacturers of high-quality vulcanised seals, supporting both standard and custom sealing solutions for defence and industrial applications.  SealFast

Name: Pollux
Description:
Pollux is a junior initiative that aims to be a reference in student involvement in the space sector. It was born from the urge to potentialize Portugal and build a bridge between the academic world and the space sector for those who are passionate and want to build a career in this field. 
Applying academic knowledge acquired in the projects and challenges of Pollux,  we help young talents develop essential features to the space sector while giving them the opportunity to work in projects and technologies linked to space. 
Our main focus is the design and conception of space technologies, thermal and structural analysis, data analysis and processing.

Name: Zeido Technologies, LLC - A Certified Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business SDVOSB
Description:
“Zeido Technologies, LLC is a global provider of Link 16 support and custom data-link equipment and secure communication solutions offering competitive defense solutions pricing and one of the quickest times for delivery of custom solutions. ”

ZEIDO has established an expert reputation for Link 16 support and products by teaming with valued and trusted partners to provide lower-cost, cost-effective solutions with a proven small business team.

Founded in 2016, ZEIDO is a certified by CVE - Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) Partner with production operations in Latrobe, PA and headquarters in the Washington DC Metro area (NOVA).

Name: Prism Defence
Description:
Prism Defence is a technology company specialising in ship-air integration.
 
Based in Adelaide, South Australia, with an office in the UK, we are a team of embarked aviation specialists and aeronautical and software engineers. Prism Defence designs, develops and supports solutions in two key areas. We deliver and support Ship Helicopter Operating Limits (SHOL) trials using our innovative procedures and systems, and develop and provide software solutions to support the entire embarked aviation continuum. 

Our core operational software is AirHub, an Operations and Embarked Aviation Management System. AirHub transitions embarked aviation operations from the manual process, procedurally complicated and data intensive legacy systems into capability-led, intelligent, digitised solutions that enhance situational awareness, inform decision making, reduce workload, and increase safety and performance. 

Designed to support progressive development, and already operating at sea, we continue to expand AirHub capabilities to realise user-driven requirements. 

Prism Defence’s solutions are applicable for all aircraft and UAS whether onboard a light-crewed Offshore Patrol Vessel, a complex, high tempo, warship, or an aircraft carrier.

Name: Berg – An HDT Global Company
Description:
Founded in 1883, Berg is recognized as a leading provider of premier camp solutions with a reputation for quality, innovation and customer service.

In 2019, Berg was acquired by HDT Global – the leader in soft wall shelter manufacturing – and added their rigid-wall shelter expertise to HDT’s line of soft wall shelter systems, environmental control and power solutions, missile transport, robotics and unmanned systems as well as commercial aerospace and industrial thermal management.

Name: Adams Arms
Description:
Adams Arms didn't invent the AR15, we just made it better.  By utilizing our patented piston system, hot carbon and gas do not come in contact with critical moving parts and components.  This results in much less maintenance and fewer malfunctions than the standard AR platform.

The Adams Arms Piston System provides superior performance and has lower maintenance costs than anything available on the market today. 

The Retro-fit System offered by Adams Arms saves Law Enforcement, Military and Private Citizens thousands of dollars by offering a simple upgrade option, instead of the high cost of replacing their current firearms.

Name: HM Elektronikai, Logisztikai és Vagyonkezelő Zrt.
Description:
A HM El Zrt. 100%-ban magyar állami tulajdonú, profitorientált  nagyvállalat. Mint a hazai védelmi ipar vezető vállalata, minősített NATO beszállítóként törekszünk egyedi megoldások és high tech műszaki fejlesztések, technológiák kidolgozására. 

Stratégiai célkitűzéseinket a haderőfejlesztés és a védelmi ipari fejlesztési program határozza meg.
A Társaság rendszerintegrátori szerepben a védelem ipar fejlődésének motorja.

Magyarország egyik legnagyobb munkáltatójaként, feladatainkat kiváló szakemberekkel, Budapesten és országszerte több mint 200 telephelyen, 4000 főt meghaladó létszámmal végezzük.

Működésünket több üzletág határozza meg, informatika, légijárművek flotta üzemeltetése, ingatlan-üzemeltetés és beruházás, vagyonvédelmi szolgáltatások, könnyűipari kapacitás (textil üzemek).

•	Szövegbányász és multimédia elemző rendszerek
•	Kiterjesztett valóság (AR) alapú fejlesztések

•	Rendszerintegrációhoz köthető szoftverfejlesztés
•	Konstruktív katonai szimulátorok üzemeltetése és támogatása
•	Hardverközeli programozás, beágyazott rendszerek fejlesztése és integrálása

Name: AstroNetix
Description:
AstroNetix is a veteran-owned and operated company providing mission-focused solutions across government, defense, and logistics sectors. Our team combines decades of real-world experience in systems engineering, cybersecurity, and program management, with a track record of supporting high-impact programs from initial concept through operational deployment.

As former stakeholders ourselves, having led billion-dollar defense programs, managed interagency technical teams, and operated in some of the most demanding mission environments, we understand what's at stake. This gives us a unique advantage: we don't just serve the mission; we've lived it. That perspective drives everything we do, from system architecture and security hardening to rapid integration and program execution.

Our solutions are designed to meet the evolving needs of today's operational landscape. Whether supporting logistics operations, enhancing space-based sensor systems, securing complex networks, or managing end-to-end program delivery, AstroNetix brings agility, technical depth, and accountability to every project. We embed with your teams, align with your priorities, and deliver with precision.

We proudly partner with U.S. defense, intelligence, and federal agencies to help streamline operations, reduce risk, and ensure mission success. At our core, we are builders, integrators, and advocates for the warfighter and mission owners, committed to delivering results that matter.

Name: DKF - European Network for Offset, Counter Trade and Industrial Participation
Description:
Our Mission & Objective

DKF is a charitable organization focusing on Industrial Participation/Offset related topics for international companies. As such it offers:

* International platform for network, know-how and experience exchange
* Encouragement of governance, compliance and transparency
* Association work
       - dialogue with experts, public authorities, international organizations
       - dialogue and collaboration with other professional associations
       - improvement of the perception of offset
* Provision of subject and industry specific information and news
* Enhancement of education and training as well as specific development of young professionals (for instance by cooperating with universities)

DKF Vision & Mission:

* To be the premier European Network for Offset, Countertrade and Industrial Participation
* To build up global membership community of Industrial Participation/ Offset Professionals
* To act as a think-tank for Offset, Counter Trade and Industrial Participation
* To be a platform offering training and advise to newcomers in the Offset,  Counter Trade and Industrial Participation community

DKF Objectives

* Support our members by offering appropriate lectures and discussion podia as well as networking opportunities
* Support members in the handling and management of their Offset/Industrial Participation commitments by sharing practical know-how and knowledge at conferences and workshops organized by DKF without becoming involved in the Member’s business and projects. DKF has no political mandate
* Share relevant information and best practice in the field of Offset, Counter Trade and Industrial participation
* Fostering of Education/Training
* Conduction of conferences within the framework of national laws in compliance with Ethics & Compliance Regulation

Our team of experienced engineers, account managers, commercial and shipping staff aim to offer an efficient "one stop shop" for our customers' materiel needs.

Name: Frontier Performance Polymers Corporation
Description:
Frontier Performance Polymers Corporation (Frontier) is a technology-driven interdisciplinary company capable of integrating R&D, system design, lightweight material development, component molding, material testing, prototype fabrication and manufacturing expertise with state-of-the-art computer modeling and simulation technology for the defense, energy, environmental, and manufacturing markets. Frontier has a 5,000 square feet research development facility located at the Picatinny Technology Innovation Center within the Picatinny Arsenal.  This ideal location allows Frontier to bridge the gap between industry and military application-oriented research and development. Frontier partners with industry, academia and government to create practical and cost-effective system solutions that address cost, performance, functionality and productivity across the entire product life cycle. 

Frontier’s technical expertise spans polymers and composites, and maintains extensive modeling capabilities and databases on materials, processing, tooling and machinery. Building on more than two decades of experience in cutting-edge product designs and solutions and successfully commercializing novel products for automotive, industrial, energy and defense markets, Frontier continues to lead in developing "next generation" cost-effective lightweight solutions to today's challenges to product development.

Name: Infrared Imaging, LLC
Description:
Your independent source for leading-edge solutions.

Founded in 2017, the professionals at Infrared Imaging LLC have worked in the infrared imaging industry for decades.  With over 100 years of combined experience, Infrared Imaging LLC partners with both customers and suppliers to solve the most demanding imaging and measurement applications. These include science and research, machine vision, target signature & tracking, surveillance, detection, and aerial imaging, along with countless others.

We empower our individual and corporate members to create value for their organizations, ultimately fortifying the future of national security.
 
Our Values:
• Professionalism: We hold ourselves and our members to the highest standards.
• Ethics and Integrity: Trust is paramount. We act with honesty and transparency.
• Focus on Results and Creating Value: We deliver solutions that make a difference.
• Agility and Flexibility: We adapt to the ever-changing needs of defense finance.
• Team Growth and Development: We invest in our members' success.
• Visionary Leadership: We guide the profession towards a brighter future.
 
Your Competency, Community, and Content Partner
The SDFM provides a comprehensive platform to propel your defense financial management (DFM) career. We offer:
 • The Certified Defense Financial Manager (CDFM) credential: The gold standard for expertise in DFM.
 • Unparalleled Community: Network with like-minded professionals at events like the annual Professional Development Institute (PDI), Data Analytics & Decision Support Virtual Conference (DA/DS), and Program/Budget Summit (P/BS).
• Invaluable Content: Stay informed with resources like the Armed Forces Comptroller (AFC) Journal, The Business of Defense video podcast, All Things Financial Management audio podcast, and task force initiatives on audit and PPBE reform.
 
Join us and build a secure future for yourself and our nation's defense.

Name: Mectron EIC
Description:
A Mectron foi fundada em 14 de fevereiro de 1991, tendo nascido em um momento em que o país, e em especial a cidade de São José dos Campos, passava por uma profunda crise. A empresa nasceu da associação de engenheiros oriundos das empresas e instituições de tecnologia do segmento aeroespacial de São José dos Campos.

Apesar da situação de profunda recessão, a empresa procurou, desde o seu início, oportunidades nas áreas em que o conhecimento de seus especialistas,pudesse ser útil. 

Logo após a sua fundação a Mectron foi contratada pela DACM (atual DSAM), órgão da Marinha Brasileira então responsável pela escolha dos fornecedores para o programa de modernização das fragatas classe Niterói visando capacitá-las para a defesa anti-aérea contra ataques de mísseis voando a baixa altitude. 


Na época a Marinha tinha ofertas de três consórcios internacionais para a realização do trabalho, tendo sido sub-contratada à Mectron uma análise técnica de cada proposta, no sentido de determinar qual se adequaria melhor aos requisitos da Marinha Brasileira. Para a realização deste trabalho foi desenvolvido um software capaz de representar com realismo cenários de combate e toda a seqüência de eventos envolvidos, desde a detecção dos alvos, a designação das armas, o acionamento das contra medidas, o lançamento de mísseis de defesa e o disparo dos canhões, até a neutralização da ameaça ou seu eventual impacto na fragata. 


Paralelamente a empresa se esforçou na busca de outros clientes, com ênfase nas áreas de automação industrial e controle de tráfego veicular, nas quais prestou serviços a empresas como Jonhson & Jonhson e General Motors, além de desenvolver um controlador eletrônico de semáforos de baixo custo, posteriormente vendido a diversas cidades do estado de São Paulo, como Jacareí, Valinhos e São José dos Campos.

It addresses unmet market need by providing global coverage to empower space system operators to maximize use of their assets. 

With SpaceLink, LEO satellite operators will have low latency, always on access to data, visitors to commercial space stations will have continuous communication capability, and satellite servicers or tugs can receive maneuvering instructions any time, because one of the SpaceLink satellites is always visible from anywhere in LEO. SpaceLink will meet pent up demand for fast and affordable access to the growing flood of data available from space.

 Stellar Decisis Series – Dive into thought-provoking discussions on space law themes with our collaborative YouTube series, exploring the intricate legalities of space endeavors.

 Clerkships & Internships – Engage with our programs across various capacities, whether you’re interested in legal research, communications, or program management. We welcome diverse talents to contribute to a growing field that blends law, policy, and the cosmic quest for order beyond Earth.

 Advocacy & Outreach – Be an ambassador for space law education with us, sharing knowledge within academic and professional communities and shaping conversations about the cosmic legal landscape.

 Partnerships & Collaborations – As sponsors of the Annual Galloway Symposium and regional organizers for the Manfred Lachs Moot Competition, we foster academic discourse and celebrate milestones in space law and policy.

Join us at the Space Court Foundation, where law meets the cosmos. Together, we are charting the course for just and equitable governance of space activities. Connect, collaborate, and contribute to the celestial narrative of tomorrow.

The command was established on December 15, 2010 to operate and oversee the Navy Regional Maintenance Centers (RMCs), to include subordinate detachments, as they execute U.S. Navy surface ship sustainment, maintenance, and modernization. Subordinate commands include:
‒	Forward Deployed Regional Maintenance Center (Naples, Italy) 
o	Forward Deployed Regional Maintenance Center Detachment (Rota, Spain) 
o	Forward Deployed Regional Maintenance Center Detachment (Manama, Bahrain) 
‒	Mid-Atlantic Regional Maintenance Center (Norfolk, VA)
‒	Southeast Regional Maintenance Center (Mayport, FL)
‒	Southwest Regional Maintenance Center (San Diego, CA)

Additionally, CNRMC collaboratively executes its surface ship support mission in partnership with NAVSEA’s Directorate for Logistics, Maintenance & Industrial Operations (SEA 04) at the following locations:
‒	Hawaii Regional Maintenance Center (Pearl Harbor Naval Shipyard and Intermediate Maintenance Facility)
‒	Northwest Regional Maintenance Center (Puget Sound Naval Shipyard and Intermediate Maintenance Facility)
‒	U.S. Naval Ship Repair Facility and Japan Regional Maintenance Center (SRF-JRMC)

Altogether, the command is responsible for partnering with private industry to execute surface ship maintenance and modernization availabilities, conduct surface fleet intermediate level repairs and total ship readiness assessments, and serves as the first responder for surface ship request for technical assistance.  

With a force of approximately 7,500 personnel across all commands, CNRMC provides around the globe support to keep the Navy’s warships operational and materially ready for action.
