Name: von Hoerner & Sulger GmbH
Description:
von Hoerner & Sulger GmbH is a privately owned SME based in Germany. The core business of vH&S is the development of new instruments for space applications. Since 1971, vH&S has built and successfully flown in space more than 15 scientific space experiments and more than 30 sounding rocket experiments.

With a staff of 23 employees, the company has a lean and efficient structure. The key to our success is our technical competence and close cooperation with our international customers and partners. Even though our contribution may be limited to a subsystem, we always strive to understand the overall system, in order to find the right answer instead of settling for a solution, which may be compliant to the requirements, but far from the overall goal. Our passion is enthusiasm for elegant engineering.

vH&S has been building several instruments to analyse the chemical composition of dust in space, which may come from several souces inside and outside our solar system. Projects that fall under this category includes the COSIMA spectrometer on ESA’s ROSETTA mission, which analyzed the chemical composition and the size of the dust particles from the comet Churyumov-Gerasimenko. Another example in this category was the CIDA instrument for the NASA STARDUST mission, sampling interplanetary dust.

Our LAD instruments for the US TWINS missions took Lyman-α measurements of the Earth’s corona, and our AC current source provides the electrical stimulus for the PK4HV experiment on the ISS, which observes the formation of plasma chrystals in the absence of the gravitational force.

We have also worked on projects such as the ExoMars Rover prototype and the micro-rover NANOKHOD for ESA’s technology and research programme.

ICARUS is a project to follow the movements of animals, especially birds across the globe with tiny transmitters. We have build the transceiver which is currently being commissioned on board of the ISS.

We welcome you to visit https://vh-s.de to see more

Name: Chris Hadfield Inc.
Description:
A moustache can tell you a lot about a man. When properly administered, it can say, “this man has commanded spacecraft”, “this man escorted Soviet bombers out of Canadian airspace”, or “this man lived in a research vessel at the bottom of the ocean.” These can be tall orders to live up to – having a moustache is a big responsibility.

“The first Canadian to walk in space.”

Col. Chris Hadfield has spent a lifetime living up to that responsibility. On July 20, 1969, when he quietly began his mission towards becoming an astronaut, the gap between being a young boy on an Ontario corn farm and the first Canadian to walk in space was unbridgeable. Canada had no astronaut program, nor would it for the foreseeable future. Chris stuck to it. He spent his time at home learning mechanics on the tractors and old cars, flying with his father and brothers every chance he could. Enrolling in air cadets, he worked his way up through the RCAF, becoming an experimental test pilot and flying over 70 types of aircraft. He made certain that when the opportunity arose, he would be prepared for it. In 1995, Chris Hadfield rode his first rocket.

Col. Hadfield continued striving. He flew again in 2001, installing Canadarm2. He served as Chief of Robotics, CapCom, and NASA’s operation in Russia, eventually going on to pilot a Russian Soyuz. The first Canadian commander of the International Space Station, a New York Times bestselling author, YouTube sensation and truly engaging speaker, Chris has worked hard to earn the right to wear his moustache.

For all peaking engagements please contact: info@chrishadfield.ca

● Firmware/ BSP Geliştirme

 Elektronik Ürün Geliştirme

Yazılım, Donanım, Mekanik tasarım konularında bir sistem sağlayıcı olarak hizmet vermekte aynı zamanda partnerimizin sahip olduğu yüksek kalitede üretim tesisleri sayesinde prototip aşamasından seri üretime kadar müşterilerimize yardımcı olmaktayız. 

● Proje Yönetimi
● Elektronik Donanım Tasarımı
● Gömülü Yazılım Tasarımı
● Mekanik Tasarım
● EMC ölçümü, optimizasyonu ve danışmanlığı

 Ürün Desteği

TQ ve Mist Elektronik olarak satış sonrası pek çok destek hizmeti de sunuyoruz.

 Elektronik Donanım Üretim Hizmetleri

Alman partnerimiz olan TQ Group bünyesinde bulunan  SMD, THT üretim alanlarında modern makina ve ekipmanlar satış ve destek hizmeti verdiğimiz modüllerin üretiminde ve özel donanımların seri üretiminde kullanılmaktadır.

 Danışmanlık Hizmetleri

Elektronik donanım tasarımı ve gömülü yazılım konularında medikal cihazlar, savunma, otomasyon sistemleri gibi çeşitli sektörlerde yönelik ürün geliştirme  danışmanlık hizmetleri sağlamaktayız.

We serve a range of clients, from startups to established enterprises, aiding their endeavors to operate within the bounds of space law. As we continue to evolve our platform, our commitment remains to provide an informative and efficient compliance experience.

Name: Microwave Tube Research and Development Centre, DRDO

The Centre is also developing multi-beam klystrons (MBK)/multi beam TWTs and microwave power modules (MPM) and MPM/MBK based transmitters for compact strategic systems. The Centre is also working on high power microwave sources, mm-wave Gyro devices and cathodes. The centre, having established a strong base in the design and development of microwave tubes, TWTs and cathodes, some of which have already been productionised, has ventured in the area of microwave power modules and transmitters.

It is an ISO 9001:2015 certified laboratory of DRDO.

Our Micro Weather Service for Aviation provides high resolution weather nowcasts used to predict the development and trajectories of severe weather objects. We have supported fielding of complex IT solutions for U.S. Army instrumented training utilizing the latest IT capabilities. The Home-Station Instrumentation System (HITS) supports collective maneuver training for platoon through battalion units.  HITS captures Live Instrumentation events, training range video and tactical voice and augments virtual and constructive simulations to improve Warfighter performance. HITS leverages VMware vCenter Server 5 and vSphere Enterprise Plus 5.0 products to virtualize the system and provide a high availability architecture that minimizes down time and data loss on the core application servers.

OSCorp is an in-space services company at the vanguard of cementing U.S. space supremacy to safeguard the space domain and build a thriving in-space economy. We develop cutting-edge technologies at the nexus of GN&C, AI/ML, LiDAR sensors, space robotics, and lasercom terminals.

OSCorp builds the next generation of autonomous spacecraft Command and Control (C2) and Space Battle Management (SBM) capabilities to protect & defend our critical national security and commercial space assets from orbital threats, enabling more precise, time sensitive, and robust Space Domain Awareness (SDA), Non-Earth Imaging (NEI), and Rendezvous Proximity Operations and Docking (RPOD).

OSCorp's technology enables the identification, tracking, and custody of potential Anti-Satellite (ASAT) launches and Uncorrelated Tracks (UCTs), as well as the inspection and salvaging of uncooperative objects using fuel-optimal maneuvers. To that end, OSCorp is working on 3 complementary projects—LIBRA, ShARCS, and PHALANX— to deliver an end-to-end solution for space security and superiority.

Name: Techaya

TECHAYA is a leading designer, developer and manufacturer of rugged MIL-STD IP-based communications solutions for ground, air and naval applications. It is part of Enercon Technologies group, designing and menufactoring power and networking solutions for the military industry. Our complete line of Ethernet switches, routers, USB hubs and media converters/network taps are designed from PCB up to meet the extreme environmental, quality and SWAP-C requirements for dismounted soldiers, both large and small unmanned systems, fixed and rotary aircraft, AFVs, missiles and naval vessel communication platforms.

Techaya was the first company in the MIL-Aero industry to deliver an ultra-compact Ethernet switch back in 2010. Today the company continues to deliver innovative COTS and customized MIL-STD packaged and ruggedized board-level solutions for military and industrial applications where reduction of size and weight of platforms and systems is top priority. Techaya's solutions are the most compact solutions of its kind and the company is committed to continue its innovation to manage higher data rates and the growing number of ports and interfaces required on military and aerospace platforms.

Techaya’s products have been widely adopted and deployed worldwide, including NATO Forces, IDF and others.

We place great emphasis on providing competitive cost/performance solutions with an emphasis on reliability and innovation to meet both industry and military standards. Techaya is an active member in Military Vectronics Association (MILVA) which establishes NATO networking standards for Vectronics application, which has evolved into the DEFSTAN-23-09 GVA, the NATO equivalent to the Victory Standard in the USA. All our manufacturing facilities are ISO9001:2015 and AS9100D certified. All our products are built according to J-STD-001 Class-3 standards.

The 110th Attack Wing is a preeminent multi-domain ANG Wing providing MQ-9, Cyber Defense, Agile Combat Support, Command & Control, and Plans for Combatant Commanders and Civil Authorities.

The 110th Attack Wing, located at Battle Creek Air National Guard Base in Battle Creek, Michigan, provides creative solutions to international and domestic challenges by applying Remotely Piloted Aircraft, Operational Plans, Command and Control, and Cyber capabilities for the 21st Century. The 110th Attack Wing is comprised of the 110th Operations Group, providing exemplary Intelligence Surveillance Reconnaissance (ISR) to various Federal and State entities anywhere in the world with the MQ-9 “Reaper” Remotely Piloted Aircraft (RPA), and the 217th Air Operation Group, which provides combat ready personnel to support the command and control of U.S. Air Forces in Europe/U.S. Air Forces Africa forces as well as aiding any emergency response with the State of Michigan. The 110th Attack Wing has seen 70 years, 11 commanders, 7 types of aircraft, and 6 major reorganizations. The 110th Attack Wing traces its lineage through on of its component units: the 172nd Attack Squadron, which earned federal recognition on September 16, 1947.

Name: LAR Manufacturing, Inc., LAR Grizzly Firearms
Description:
Headquartered in West Jordan Utah, LAR Manufacturing has been producing precision engineered firearms and firearms components for over 40 years.  Founded in 1968, LAR began manufacturing of M16 upper receivers for the Rock Island U.S. Armament Command; LAR won other government contracts such as the M3 Tripod Mounts for the M2 machine gun as well as the M85 Barrel extension subassembly, and the M240 machine gun.  Due to our machining capabilities, LAR launched the Grizzly Mark 1 handgun, “The Browning 1911 on steroids” as it was coined for so many years.  Due to our expertise in precision machining and firearms, LAR created the GRIZZLY BIG BOAR .50BMG rifle over 20 years ago. LAR has since launched a new .50BMG Tactical Rifle.
 
LAR never stopped building firearms components for the M16 and AR15 platforms.  LAR has acted and continues to be a contract manufacturing facility for some of the world’s largest AR15 brand names in the industry.  LAR has continued its Research and Development and developed a precision machined side charged upper assembly that makes the AR15 platform more functional, durable, accurate for right and left charged models as well as ambidextrous models.
 
LAR Manufacturing continues to innovate and expand. Skilled employees work in a multi-million dollar, state-of-the-art manufacturing complex covering over 35,000 square feet. Research and development is mission critical and exciting new products are on the drawing board. Indeed, the future is bright at LAR Manufacturing.

Misyonumuz; bilimsel ve yenilikçi bir anlayışla savunma, havacılık, güvenlik, savunma sanayi ve teknoloji alanlarında araştırmalar yapmak, projeler geliştirmek, eğitim, danışmanlık ve temsilcilik hizmetleri sunmak, gerektiğinde ülkemizin önceliklerine uygun şekilde uluslararası işbirliği faaliyetlerini desteklemektir.

Faaliyet Alanlarımız:

Tedarik: Savunma ve havacılık alanında ihtiyaç duyulan çeşitli malzemelerin tedarik projelerini yürütüyoruz.

Eğitim: Yetkin eğitmenlerimizle bireysel ve kurumsal eğitim taleplerinizi karşılıyoruz. 

Danışmanlık: Savunma, savunma sanayi, güvenlik ve havacılık alanlarında danışmanlık hizmeti sunuyoruz.

HRI has been intensely focused on pressure-sensitive munitions since the beginning, and has taken a holistic and scientific approach to mitigating the threats of landmines and pressure-sensitive improvised explosive devices, (IEDs). Name: Hacking for Defense Stanford
Description:
Hacking for Defense (H4D) is a graduate course founded at Stanford University in 2016. Multi-disciplinary student teams learn the Lean Startup methodology and work with active Defense Department and Intelligence Community personnel to develop solutions for real-world problems. The H4D course is now taught at more than 55 schools in the United States and in four countries. It’s a program of the National Security Innovation Network (NSIN), powered by BMNT, Inc., and the Common Mission Project. Hacking for Defense at Stanford University is an effort of the Precourt Institute for Energy and sponsored by the Office of Naval Research (ONR).

The Hacking for Defense Initiative at Stanford University transitions academic technologies from the lab and emerging commercial products into viable defense capabilities. The H4D Initiative leverages its deep defense networks to align new technologies and products with critical national security needs. The Initiative also serves as a forum for collaboration by gathering government personnel (e.g. operators, engineers, researchers, and scientists), industry personnel (e.g. entrepreneurs, innovators, companies), and university academics to tackle the world’s toughest challenges.

Name: The Parachute Regiment (Special Forces)
Description:
The Parachute Regiment (Special Forces) was the brain child of Lt Col Megh Singh who created the "Meghdoot Force" in 1965. This was later merged with "The Parachute Regiment" in 1966 to form the first Commando Battalion  called the 9 Para Commando Battalion and then in 1967, 10 Para Commando Battalion. 

This was the first time ever Commando Battalion were raised and then in 2001 all Commando Battalions were         re-desginated as Special Forces Battalion due to change in the mandate. 

Today the Parachute Regiment (Special Forces) is the punch line of the Indian Armed Forces along with other specialised units from the Indian Navy (Marine Commandos) and Indian Air Force (Garuds). The Parachute Regiment Special Forces also contribute to many other specialised units like National Security Guards and Special Group. 

This is a closed group is dedicated to all those brave souls who have laid down their life for the man standing next to him, his country and to all those who have served in Para (Special Forces) of The Parachute Regiment.

Name: National Military Intelligence Foundation
Description:
The National Military Intelligence Foundation (NMIF) was organized to advance public awareness of the roles and contributions intelligence professionals make to society, military intelligence organizations, disciplines, and enhancements in analytical methods, and techniques.  Building on a long history of success as the National Military Intelligence Association, which was established in 1974, NMIF came to corporate life as an IRS Code 501(c)(3) public charity on August 7, 2007. 

Mission
NMIF is dedicated to inspiring new generations of intelligence professionals, recognizing the success of current intelligence professionals, and sustaining the intelligence workforce needed to ensure the overall capability of the military intelligence function to support the national security of the United States of America.  

Core Values:
- Advocating for intelligence and national security professionals

- Inspiring university students and junior intelligence professionals to pursue careers in intelligence

- Providing national-level recognition of military intelligence and national security professionals

-  Supporting collaboration among the Intelligence Community, academia, and national security  organizations to promote careers and sustain the intelligence and national security professions

- Encouraging the professional development of military intelligence practitioners to facilitate and develop core capabilities in the intelligence disciplines

Philosophy:
The National Military Intelligence Foundation (NMIF) believes in the power of the intelligence mission to inspire young people to join the intelligence profession as a career of service  to the nation.  NMIF continuously engages current and future intelligence professionals, organizations, industry, and academic institutions to contribute to the overall sustainment of the U.S. military intelligence workforce.

Name: Interstellar Technologies
Description: Interstellar is a small, veteran-owned business focused on providing innovations in design and manufacturing for propulsion and space systems. The Interstellar team has previously worked several high-profile Government and Commercial space programs which have included the SpaceX Falcon 9 launch vehicle and the SpaceX Dragon capsule.  In addition, the Interstellar team has experience stemming from many rocket engine development and flight programs including the Space Shuttle Main Engine, Air Force Integrated Powerhead Demonstrator (IPD) engine, NASA J-2X engine, SpaceX Merlin engine, SpaceX Raptor engine, SpaceX Draco and SuperDraco, and the Paul Allen PGA Engine program. The team at Interstellar completed two years of advanced development for the Stratolaunch 200,000 pound thrust hydrogen PGA Engine, which had completed several key design and hardware testing milestones which would have enabled one of the fastest large engine developments in history.

Name: Daronmont Technologies
Description:
Daronmont is a Small to Medium Enterprise (SME) operating mainly in the Defence Electronics sector integrating and supporting Surveillance Systems, Command and Control applications, Deployable C4I systems and ESM/DF systems for aerospace, land and naval applications.  The company designs, develops, manufactures, installs, operates and supports both hardware and software systems, system interfaces and system upgrades.  In hardware, Daronmont covers the entire electronics spectrum and in software, from databases to real time embedded software.  Daronmont has approximately 90 professional engineers and para-professionals.  The company’s experience is diverse – from building prototypes to support of unattended communications/surveillance facilities.  Since undertaking the first task for Defence in 1998 Daronmont has completed over 100 discreet defence projects in addition to its major contracts.  Daronmont is successfully exporting systems to New Zealand and Canada.

Passive Radar commercialisation is an area of intense focus as we deliver prototype systems to the Australian Defence Forces via the Defence Innovation Hub.

Name: Operation EUNAVFOR MED IRINI
Description:
Operation EUNAVFOR MED IRINI is a military Common Security and Defence Policy (CSDP) operation.

IRINI, (Greek for "peace"), will have as its core task the implementation of the UN arms embargo through the use of aerial, satellite and maritime assets. 

In particular the mission will be able to carry out inspections of vessels on the high seas off the coast of Libya suspected to be carrying arms or related material to and from Libya in accordance with United Nations Security Council Resolution  2292 (2016).

As secondary tasks, Operation EUNAVFOR MED IRINI will also:
- monitor and gather information on illicit exports from Libya of petroleum, crude oil and refined petroleum products;
- contribute to the capacity building and training of the Libyan Coast Guard and Navy in law enforcement tasks at sea;
- contribute to the disruption of the business model of human smuggling and trafficking networks through information gathering and patrolling by planes.

IRINI will be led by Rear Admiral Fabio Agostini as EU Operation Commander, and its headquarters will be located in Rome, Italy.

We inform our followers about our Netiquette, an important document, which allows a fair interaction in social networks, obviously in respect of Social Network’s Declaration of Rights.

For more information regarding membership please email: info@oklahomadefense.com .

Name: NDIA Michigan Chapter
Description:
The Michigan Chapter of the National Defense Industrial Association (NDIA) was founded in 1919 as the Army Ordnance Association. Its charter is to provide a legal and ethical forum for the interchange of ideas between the government and industry to resolve industrial problems of joint concern.

Currently the Michigan Chapter has more than 1,700 members representing government, military, and industry. The majority of large businesses that support the Michigan defense community are Corporate Members of NDIA. The Michigan Chapter is involved in a myriad of different activities that support all branches of the armed forces, local, state, and federal government, as well as the corporate entities that support our warfighters, first responders, and patriotic Americans.

VISION:
NDIA is the trusted leader in defense and national security associations.

MISSION:
NDIA engages thoughtful and innovative leaders to promote the best policies, practices, products and technology for warfighters and others who ensure the safety and security of our nation.

• CHAMPION issues that contribute to the strength, resiliency and capacity of the industrial base
•  BUILD a vigorous, responsive and collaborative community in support of defense and national security
•  CONVENE legal and ethical forums for exchange of ideas, information, viewpoints and capabilities

Name: Simthetiq
Description:
Bringing Simulation and Training Ever Closer to Reality

Since 2005, Simthetiq has been helping trainees improve their performance and safety through outstanding content for the Armed Forces and the Civil Aviation fields. We have acquired expertise and understanding to develop, support and bring solutions to our clients.

Name: TTI Ray-Q
Description:
Ray-Q, was established in 1969 . The partnership with the customers in the design and supply of high Quality electrical interconnect solutions has earned the company a leading status with the military, aerospace and other high reliability products industries. In 2000, Ray-Q was incorporated as a representative, consultant and distributor of Tyco Electronics, Raychem Division, for Israel, Turkey, Eastern & parts of Central Europe, and India.

Design and documentation services electrical wiring and harnessing Prototype assembly line.

On site Customer training and installations.

Among its major customers are: The Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI), Elbit, Israel MOD,IMI, Rafael and all other major aerospace and defense companies.

In March 2010, TTI acquired Net-Aye Components to establish its first dedicated office in Israel. Net-aye Components was founded on 1998 as a part of the companies Bar-Tec Ltd and Tel-Ad Electronics Ltd that together formed one of the major distributors of interconnect components in the Israeli market.

In October 2013, TTI Inc., an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway, announced the acquisition of Ray-Q Interconnect. 

The two companies, Ray-Q and Net-Aye have merged to a single company in January 2015 under the name of TTI Ray-Q.

Name: Hyperspace Challenge
Description:
Tech Startups + Defense Innovators = Accelerated Innovation.

Hyperspace Challenge, powered by the Air Force Research Lab New Mexico and ABQid, brings together tech startups with defense innovators to accelerate innovation for the defense community. Our unique program facilitates interactions between promising technologies and timely problems to increase rapid acquisition and contracting opportunities. Our goal isn’t innovation at the speed of light; it’s faster.

U.S. Tech Startups: You’re excited about the technology you’ve developed–you even have commercial traction. You’re sure it could apply to the space domain to solve defense problems, but you’re not sure who to approach, what opportunity to apply for, or what jargon to use. This program is for you.

We’re putting the best data analytics startups in front of top defense innovators with the ability to influence purchasing decisions. Our goal is to provide startups with an amazing customer discovery experience that results in contracting and acquisitions.

AFCEA Hawaii provides a forum for the ethical exchange of information between our military, government, industry, and academia communities to collaborate so that technology and strategy align with the needs of these communities.

AFCEA is an organization that brings government, industry and academia together to share technical expertise and address community needs; we enable military, government, industry and academic collaboration. AFCEA Hawaii is for anyone in Hawaii with interests in STEM, Cyber, or gaining a better
perspective of all levels of government in the Pacific. We welcome all military and professionals in both industry and government, as well as students and their instructors in academia.

We aim to facilitate networking and engagement, host informative presentations, develop students and professionals, and give back to the local community and nation. AFCEA is a place to reach out and ask a question, state a need, or offer an idea; it is an approachable, professional environment that feels like Ohana.

In addition to our government and military offerings, we provide open-enrollment tactical training for civilians who seek professional-level instruction in firearms handling, home defense, and advanced shooting techniques.

Redback One remains committed to innovation and excellence—continuously refining our methodologies to stay ahead of evolving threats and deliver cutting-edge, battle-proven training solutions.

Our team’s passion is seen every day, whether it’s in designing a new technology solution, building a robust, policy-compliant IT system, or stress-testing a system to identify gaps and security vulnerabilities. We are passionate about technology, we respect our nation’s security needs and we understand the nuances, demands and critical nature of delivering the right outcomes. If you possess a love for country, solving complex technical challenges and learning new technologies, we invite you to explore opportunities with us. Aquila has been providing Information Technology and Cyber Solutions since 1987.

See how our machine learning and software technologies can change the paradigm for your organization.

Name: EDePro d.o.o
Description:
Company EDePro (Engine Development and Production) is today regional leader in propulsion systems solutions for solid rockets, turbo jet powered missiles and production of energetic materials.

EDePro has a long tradition. The foundations of the Company were laid more than twenty years ago, when the Laboratory for Jet Propulsion was founded in 1985. at the Faculty of Mechanical Engineering at the University of Belgrade. Name: ProActive Technologies, LLC
Description:
NOTICE OF DISSOLUTION
Pursuant to the laws of the Commonwealth of Viginia, Pro-Active Technologies, Inc. (the “Corporation”) has filed Articles of Dissolution with the State Corporation Commission.
The Commonwealth of Virginia State Corporation Commission has determined that the Articles of Dissolution comply with all applicable legal requirements and has ordered that a Certificate of Dissolution be issued and recorded, along with the Articles of Dissolution, in the Office of the Clerk of the Commission, effective June 24, 2025.

As of the effective date, the Corporation has ceased operations and is in the process of winding up its affairs in accordance with Virginia law.

Articles of Termination

Upon completion of the winding-up process and the satisfaction of all known liabilities and obligations, the Corporation will file Articles of Termination with the State Corporation Commission pursuant to Virginia Code § 13.1-745. The filing of the Articles of Termination will formally terminate the Corporation’s legal existence.
The Corporation is on track for filing the Articles of Termination no later than 30 December 2025.

For any inquiries related to outstanding obligations or claims, please contact the following:

Name: EIDEL AS
Description:
EIDEL has designed and delivered products and control systems to the aerospace, military and industrial markets since 1966. System definition of hardware and software designs are covered. EIDEL provides standard products as well as custom designs. A key element is combining customer requirements with state-of-the-art technology into high quality products.

Defence
The business idea in the Defence activities is to supply remote control and management equipment to major communication systems primarily to the armed forces in Norway and abroad. Secure communication related to encryption and tactical data links is a significant part of the Defence activity.

Telemetry
EIDEL focus on datacollection, storage and presentation of telemetry data. The PCM Encoders can be configured by the User for advanced test applications. Solid State Recorders store data under hostile environments. The PC based PCM Telemetry systems decode and present the test data. Most applications are military, but the range from industrial to space is covered.

Space
EIDEL`s activity covers study, design and delivery of scientific instruments and data handling systems. Flight hardware has been delivered. Ground support and test equipment are the main areas.

The AES facility consists of 1,300 acres in McEwen, TN, fifty miles west of Nashville on Interstate 40. The production facility consists of eight purpose built production buildings and a quality lab for product evaluation and analysis. All manufacturing facilities are environmentally controlled.

AES provides commercial and government customers manufacturing and storage capabilities for a wide range of energetic products.  Our core areas of expertise include:
-Manufacturing and processing bulk explosives
-Fabricating energetic devices
-Load, Assemble, Pack (LAP)
-Casting of specialty melt pour and cast cure explosive devices
-Pressing explosive munitions pellets
-Explosive testing and characterization

With the most advanced digitizers and FPGA-based products, 4DSP has a reputation for delivering high-performance DSP solutions for the specific requirements of Military, Aerospace, Biomedical and Chemical Science applications. As an AS9100 certified company, 4DSP excels at providing solutions that make an impact in both the lab and the field.

Platformun amacı; mevcut tedarikçilerimizle ilişkilerin güçlendirilmesi, millileştirme çalışmalarının etkinliğinin artırılması, potansiyel katma değer sağlayıcı tedarikçilerin ekosistemimize kazandırılması,sanayileşme modelinin desteklenmesi ve kazan-kazan düşüncesiyle tedarik tabanının sürdürülebilirliğine katkı sunulmasıdır.

ASELSAN’a, onaylı firma olmak için başvuru yapacak olan tedarikçiler, başvuru kapsamında tamamlanması gereken adımlara ve sonrasında izlenecek sürece ilişkin bilgilere kolayca ulaşabilecektir. 

Ayrıca yurt dışına bağımlılığın azaltılması yolunda temel misyonumuz olan millileştirme/yerlileştirme çalışmalarımıza katkı sağlayacak firmalarımız için başvuru süreci ve millileştirilecek ürün listesi bu platform ile entegre edilmiştir. Tedarikçilerimiz; etkinliklerimizden, sunulacak eğitimlerden ve farklı avantajlardan doğrudan haberdar olabilecek, raporlar, araştırmalar ve bilgilendirme yazıları ile güncel gelişmeler ve sektörün ileriye dönük vizyonu hakkında bilgi sahibi olabileceklerdir. 

Bu platform, ASELSAN ile ortak değerleri benimseyen tedarikçilerimiz ile oluşturduğumuz kıymetli ailemize güç katacaktır.

Name: Nammo Sweden AB
Description:
Nammo is an international aerospace and defense company headquartered in Norway. With more than 3 600 employees, 27 production sites and a presence in 11 countries, Nammo is today one of the world’s leading providers of specialty ammunition and rocket motors for both military and civilian customers.

Nammo Karlsborg is located next to the historical Karlsborg fortress at the western side of lake Vättern in Sweden. The ammunition production activities started in 1870 in the old Karlsborg fortress. Already in 1887 the factory was moved outside the fortress compound, where they remain to this day. Karlsborg employs around 240 people, and serves as Nammo’s primary hub for small caliber ammunition for use by assault rifles and other light weapons, including armor piercing and non-toxic/lead free ammunition.

Nammo Karlskoga is located in Björkborn, 45 km west of Örebro in Sweden. The industrial history in Björkborn dates back to the late 19th century, when a chemicals factory was established at the site. What is today Nammo Karlskoga was established in the 1970s. The facility today employs about 150 people, and is mainly focused on explosive filling and final assembly of 40 mm, 57 mm and 155 mm ammunition.

Nammo Vingåker is a demilitarization facility close to Vingåker outside Katrineholm, approx. 45 km south-east of Örebro in Sweden. The Vingåker facility has been engaged in demilitarization of ammunition for more than 40 years. The plant was established in 1967 as an assembly and explosives casting plant for the famous Swedish Carl-Gustaf system. Demilitarization operations started in early 1970’s and have from then grown to be the main activity. Today approx. 70 employees have their work at Nammo in Vingåker.

Name: Dynamit Nobel Defence GmbH
Description:
Dynamit Nobel Defence GmbH (DND) is a medium-sized technology company, mainly active in the field of defense technology. DND emerged as an independent GmbH from the Defence division of the former Dynamit Nobel AG. The company looks back on more than 60 years of successful development, qualification, production and service support of various explosive-based active and protective systems, priority for military and government applications. The portfolio has expanded considerably over the decades and today includes not only so-called shoulder-fired weapon systems but also holistic protection technologies for land platforms, fire protection for civil and military applications, test charges, environmental and qualification services as well as research and development in several fields of technology with both military and civil applications. 
Collaborations with research and educational institutions make the company a technology hub in the region. The company headquarters and the production plant are located in Burbach, in the industrial Siegerland region about 100 km north of Frankfurt. The factory site includes more than one square kilometer and houses 260 buildings and manufacturing complexes for the development, production and testing of high-tech products.

Name: Applied Research Laboratories, The University of Texas at Austin
Description:
Applied Research Laboratories, The University of Texas at Austin (ARL:UT) advances the mission of our parent university of education, research, and public service. Through fundamental research, innovative science, and applied engineering, ARL:UT makes significant contributions in support of national security. We are proud that many of our contributions have a direct and positive impact for those who protect us through their military service.

An integral part of a top-tier research university, ARL:UT builds upon a 75-year history of world-class research and expertise for the Department of Defense and other sponsors supporting their critical and ongoing quest to maintain the technological advantage that is critical to our nation. We maintain a top-quality research staff with deep technical expertise that provides multi-disciplinary research and unbiased guidance to the government and tackles complex and challenging problems. Our strong commitment to student employment and programs provides support to the next generation of scientists and engineers and helps to shape the future of defense research.

We value diversity in our staff, in our multi-disciplinary research, and in our approach to innovation. We are committed to having an atmosphere where everyone we employ or with whom we interact is respected.

Name: CI Systems
Description:
Founded in 1977, CI Systems has grown to include an Electro-Optics Test Systems division, a semiconductor division and a subsidiary in the electronic card assembly and inspection industry. Approximately half its sales are in the USA, with the rest in Israel, Europe and the Far East.

Its Electro-Optics Test Systems division develops and commercializes accurate MIL-Standard E-O test equipment for all stages in the product life cycle. Its products range from standard off-the-shelf components to fully customized test systems.

CI Semi, its semiconductor division, offers state-of-the-art metrology solutions for the semiconductor processing industry. It develops and commercializes non-contact temperature monitors and has recently introduced a new in-line process analyzer. CI Semi’s sales now account for close to 40% of the company’s revenues. 

CI Systems maintains a wholly-owned sales and manufacturing facility (CI Systems Inc.) in the USA, a network of sales representatives worldwide, and an R&D facility in Israel. It is part of a group of companies held by CI Technologies Inc, which is a registered Delaware corporation, and is a publicly traded company (TASE).

Name: Machine Specialties, Inc.

Name: PLR Systems Pvt Ltd
Description:
PLR Systems Pvt Ltd (PLR) is a Joint Venture between Adani Group, India and Israel Weapon Industries (IWI), Israel for manufacturing of world class Small Arms in India. IWI weapons like TAVOR Assault Rifle, X95 Assault Rifle, GALIL Sniper Rifle, NEGEV Light Machine Gun and UZI Sub Machine Gun are already in service with the Indian Armed Forces, CAPFs (CRPF/ BSF/ SFF) and State Police Forces. The Tavor Assault Rifles and Galil Sniper Rifles were used by our elite Special Forces during the Surgical Strikes.

In response to the Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s clarion call of ‘Make in India’ initiative, PLR has established a State-of-the-Art Small Arms manufacturing facility at Malanpur Industrial Area, Gwalior, MP and is proud to be the first private sector company that was granted license by Govt of India for manufacturing of Small Arms and Ammunition.

PLR has begun manufacturing the complete range of these weapons at its facility and has commenced supply of weapons to multiple state police and Central Armed Police Forces. The first set of Indian Manufactured X95 Assault Rifles were manufactured from the plant in Oct 2018.

PLR has also established a Gunsmith Shop and an Opto Electronic Laboratory to carry out the Maintenance, Repair and Overhauling (MRO) of Small Arms and Weapon Sights.

Name: Boğaziçi Savunma Teknolojileri A.Ş.
Description:
BOĞAZİÇİ SAVUNMA TEKNOLOJİLERİ

Yeni bir savunma anlayışı ve teknolojisi ile, sektörü içerisinde dünyanın en iyi “Savunma Teknolojileri” şirketi olma yolunda emin adımlarla ilerlemektedir.

Savunma alanında halen kullanılmakta olan klasik ve “bilinen” ürünlerin yerine, çok hızlı ve sürekli olarak güncellenebilen ‘en yeni’ teknolojiyi kullanmak suretiyle, daha akıllı, etki alanı çok daha geniş, minimum enerji harcayan ve insan sağlığını her şeyden ön planda tutarak üretmekte olduğu savunma teknolojileri sayesinde, beraber yol aldığı iş ortaklarına sonsuz bir ‘güven’ hissi vermekte olan “Boğaziçi Savunma Teknolojileri”, düşük maliyetler ile kurumlar ve kişiler için hayati önem arz eden verilerinin ve haklarının korunmasını ilke edinmiştir.

“Boğaziçi Savunma Teknolojileri”, çözüm ve iş ortağınız olarak, alanında rakipsiz ve en yenilikçi firma olmasının yanısıra, size “güvenilir bir çözüm” sağlayacak ve tüm değerlerinizi koruyacak olan tek adrestir.

Name: Horstman UK
Description:
A company of the RENK Group, the global Horstman team combines to provide a complete mobility solution through systems engineering, design, validation, manufacture and worldwide support of our military and commercial customers. 

Horstman Defence Systems Limited is a UK based business with a vibrant export portfolio. We provide a full solution from design and production engineering , through prototyping, manufacture, assembly, test, and logistics support. Celebrating our centenary in 2013, Horstman has proven domain experience in armoured vehicle suspension and a strong capability in high integrity manufacture, a worldwide customer base and capable leadership and project teams. 

Recognised as a world leading designer and developer of military suspension, solutions range from lightweight-wheeled and air portable vehicles, through infantry and engineer vehicles up to the heaviest of Main Battle Tanks. Suspension products include: Hydrogas, Hydrostrut, InArm, Integral Rotary Dampers, Lever Arm Rotary Dampers and Running Gear and Track Tensioners. We combine the expertise of Horstman Systems Inc, Canada to offer a strong systems engineering and design capability in electronic and electro-hydraulic active ride management and height control. 

In the US - Horstman Inc, Michigan provides engineering, PM, manufacturing, assembly and test services to prime contractors and vehicle OEMs on wheeled and tracked armoured vehicles with expertise in defence hydraulics, suspension, gearboxes and systems integration. Additionally, a novel compressible fluid strut technology offers a low cost dual-use system. 

Horstman have capability in precision machining, assembly and test of mechanical, electro mechanical and hydraulic assemblies including safety critical components in high strength steels, aluminium and titanium alloys, with exacting machining tolerances and surface finishes required for the defence, sub-sea, oil and gas, aerospace and nuclear markets.

Description:

Name: Spirit Electronics
Description:
Spirit Electronics is a veteran-owned, woman-owned value-added supplier of high reliability components and superior supply chain solutions. As an award-winning, full turnkey distributor, Spirit delivers authorized products from wafer sales through fully screened, radiation and environmentally qualified components.

Our value-added services include SMI/VMI, Electrical and Environmental Testing, Design, Assembly, End-of-Life Management and more:
• Electronic component distribution, including passive components, discrete semis, memory, interconnect, RF/Microwave and electro-mechanical devices
• Fulfillment, material planning, material purchasing (full BOM), inventory management, just-in-time delivery, and supply-chain solutions
• Wafer sort, assembly, test, qualification, radiation services, and simple and complex kitting

Our engineers are experienced in high-speed digital, mixed signal, analog, RF and SOC test-program development. Our team will work seamlessly with you and your team to help meet your market challenges.

We Own the Qual

Spirit Electronics performs in-house testing to qualify our components. We take full ownership of the product and any failures up until it is received on our customers’ dock. This assures the parts received are 100% problem-free. Guaranteed.

Spirit’s History & Mission

Spirit Electronics was founded by a Native American woman and continues today as a USAF Veteran, woman owned small business. Spirit’s owner and CEO, Marti McCurdy, has served the military and space industries around the world throughout her career, starting with work on jamming radar systems for fighter jets, to being a Level 3 ultrasonic specialist performing ultrasonic inspections in more than 20 countries. 

Spirit strives to be a valued partner to key technology sectors, and our status as VOWOSB, SDB owned-company provides diversity requirement relief to Defense and Aerospace customers.

Name: SCOPEX

Scopex has been supplying state-of-the-art equipment and products in the following sectors for over 30 years:
- EOD/C-IED, bomb disposal and demolition equipment

- Ground robots
- Tactical equipment

Name: Sciaky, Inc.
Description:
Sciaky, Inc., a subsidiary of Phillips Service Industries, Inc. (PSI), is a leading provider of metal 3D printing / Additive Manufacturing (AM) solutions. Our exclusive Electron Beam Additive Manufacturing (EBAM) process is the fastest, most cost-effective 3D printing process in the market for large-scale metal parts, allowing manufacturers to save significant time and money over traditional manufacturing and prototyping processes. 

We also provide state-of-the-art electron beam (EB) welding systems and advanced arc welding systems — along with extensive job shop EB welding services — to the aerospace, defense, automotive, medical and other manufacturing industries. Our state-of-the-art equipment meets rigid military specifications to manufacture items such as airframes, landing gear, jet engines, guided missiles and vehicle parts.

Name: TBW Global
Description:
TBW Global is a specialized defense contractor with deep roots in supplying language services and cultural awareness training to the international security sector. 
 
Since it’s inception TBW Global has been a vital player in major Government contracts providing: training, staff augmentation, human resources, logistics and technology. 
We offer scalable workforce and program outsourcing solutions giving clients full visibility and control of their non-permanent workforce, including temporary workers, contractors, consultants and resources engaged under a program governed by a statement of work or task order.

Human Resources, logistics and technology all under one supplier!

Name: QED Systems, LLC
Description: QED Systems, LLC is a small business dedicated to solving client’s critical problems by applying technical, operational, and managerial excellence to provide actionable solutions.  QED Systems, LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Peerless Technologies Corporation, is a solution provider to the DoD with a focus on US Army C4ISR Research, Development, Test, and Experimentation.  QED Systems, LLC is comprised of a team of seasoned professionals headquartered at Aberdeen Proving Ground MD, and operating at APG MD, MDL NJ, Fort Huachuca AZ, and Fort Hood TX communities.  QED Systems, LLC provides engineering solutions to address US Army requirements in Systems Engineering and Integration, Information Assurance, Intel Systems, Computer Sciences, Software Development, Modeling & Simulation, Laboratory and Field Instrumentation, and Systems Analysis.

Name: Dasco Storage Solutions
Description:
Dasco Storage Solutions is a leading provider of secure weapons, computer and multi-media storage solutions worldwide. Our secure storage systems have been utilized by militaries, law enforcement agencies, government departments and private businesses around the world. 

Established in 1963, our head office is located in Ontario, Canada. This full-service facility is where we design, engineer and manufacture every detail of our storage solutions. Our experienced engineers focus on developing highly-customizable products suitable for a variety of applications. 

Dasco Storage Solutions product applications include:

Weapon storage - Our weapon storage products include our versatile integrated weapons storage platform (IWSP), heavy-duty secure cabinets, and secure racking systems. All of these solutions can be customized to store a variety of weapons and weapon accessories with Dasco Storage Solutions components. Our weapons storage solutions are the first choice of militaries and law enforcement agencies worldwide.

Computer storage - Computers, laptops, tablets and other computer devices are expensive and contain sensitive information. Securing devices, and more importantly the information on them, is easy with our line of computer storage systems. Our products included powered cabinets for laptops, computers, fax machines and even cryptography equipment. 

Secure media storage - In a world of evolving technologies, the secure, manageable and retrievable requirement of information is essential. Our media storage solutions are perfect for securing end-tab filing, hanging files, discs and other back-up media. Dasco Storage Solutions's exclusive multi-media storage cabinets and racks offer unparalleled degree of flexibility and adaptability meeting and surpass many health and safety standards (HIPAA & PIPEDA for the US & Canada).

Name: Norwegian-American Defense Industry Council
Description: The Norwegian-American Defense & Homeland Security Industry Council (NADIC) is a trade association established to develop the business and research cooperation of the Norwegian and American defense industries. It studies relevant opportunities and facilitates stronger ties between Norway and the U.S., while encouraging a fair competitive environment in U.S. markets.

Name: Faxon Machining
Description:
We’re With You 100% of The Way:
We combine a highly experienced and motivated staff of manufacturing team members with a modern, state-of-the-art, highly capable manufacturing facility to carry projects from concept through full volume production. 

By maintaining a wide variety of in house capabilities with a fully vetted and capable group of vendors we can manage complete, complex programs.  We are committed to providing the shortest time, best quality and competitive pricing to meet the needs of our customers.        

We have participated as a team member on several developmental programs recently that have produced impressive overall results, which includes concept to contract efforts in less than 1 year.

Several programs Faxon has participated in the development of have resulted in producing those products or parts in full, long-term volume.

For orders ranging from complex single piece prototypes to million piece high volume production orders, Faxon Machining has always been dedicated to serving the needs of our customers regardless of the requirements.

We are focused on providing exceptional quality through our ISO accreditations and business operations while achieving on-time delivery through the efficiencies of electronic scheduling and detailed planning practices. Our employees are the cornerstone of our business and work hard at exceeding all expectations regardless of the program or size of the order.  Above all else, we find great value in building relationships with our customer and not merely completing an order.

The executive management team is dedicated to providing the necessary resources and corporate governance to facilitate growth, continuous improvement and to ensure compliance with all applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

Name: Strategic Analysis Incorporated
Description:
Strategic Analysis Inc. (SA) is an industry-leading government professional services firm that excels in providing innovative solutions to the problems of today and tomorrow. The talents and experience that make up the SA team provide all of the skills, tools and creative ideas necessary to address the technical, operational, programmatic, and policy aspects of national defense and homeland security issues. In addition, SA was featured in the November 2007 issue of Washingtonian Magazine-Best Places to Work.

Strategic Analysis began operations in Arlington, VA in May of 1986, quickly establishing a name for itself by winning competitive contracts from the Office of Naval Research, the Office of the Secretary of Defense and the Los Alamos National Laboratory to support technical studies and key national security science and technology (S&T) programs. 

Years later, Strategic Analysis has grown substantially, with offices in Arlington, VA, Washington DC and Colorado Springs, CO. SA's core capabilities have also greatly expanded and diversified as new demands have been placed on the professional services industry. While SA's list of clients has grown, our core values of providing the highest quality support to our customers, hiring and building relationships with good people to do great things, a focus on SA as an "extended family," and working to build a better community for everyone remain the same as they were twenty years ago.

Description:
PatchPlus Consulting, Inc., is a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned and Woman-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB/WOSB) committed to making a difference to our customers, industry partners, and the Warfighter.

Since 2002, PatchPlus Consulting, Inc. has continued to make a difference to warfighters by providing world-class subject matter expertise. 100% of PatchPlus SMEs have served either on active duty or the reserve component, and some continue to serve in the Air National Guard or Air Force Reserves. From the CEO to the team in our headquarters ‘Tower’ and the 100+ employees located across the US, every employee is laser-focused on continuing to make a difference to the Warfighter. We seek opportunities where our experience and reputation for capable and credible support fulfills our collective ‘why’.

Name: Indo- Russian Rifles Private Limited
Description: Indo-Russian Rifles Private Limited (IRRPL) is a landmark joint venture between India and Russia, established in 2019 under an Inter-Governmental Agreement to manufacture 601,427 AK-203 assault rifles for the Indian Armed Forces. Rooted in decades of Indo-Russian friendship and strategic trust, IRRPL embodies the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat by producing state-of-the-art small arms entirely in India. Our advanced facility in Korwa, Amethi district (Uttar Pradesh), strategically located within the Uttar Pradesh Defence Corridor, not only supplies modern rifles but also drives MSME participation, generates skilled employment, and builds a strong local supply chain. To date, 55,000 AK-203 rifles have been delivered to the Army, with production operating at an exceptional pace of one rifle every 100 seconds. With 100% Transfer of Technology successfully absorbed from Russia, India now possesses complete capability for future upgrades, R&D, and indigenous development of small arms, ensuring both self-reliance and long-term industrial growth. Beyond the AK-203 program, IRRPL is expanding into a broader portfolio that includes carbines, light machine guns, accessories such as optics and modular gear, and forward-looking autonomous systems that align with the future of warfare. Every rifle and system we produce strengthens India’s defence preparedness, empowers soldiers with world-class equipment, and contributes to a robust and competitive domestic defence industry. With its proven efficiency, strong foundation, and commitment to innovation, IRRPL is proud to stand at the intersection of Indo-Russian cooperation and India’s industrial transformation, ensuring that the nation not only meets its current defence needs but also builds the capabilities of tomorrow.

Name: W International
Description:
W International is a full service manufacturer of large complex metal fabrications to serve the Aerospace, Automotive, Commercial, Defense, and Energy markets. The company’s highly trained welding and technical staff are able to support the requirements of working with a diverse range of materials including armor, aluminum, titanium, invar, mild and high strength steels.

W International is located in the Charleston, South Carolina area with our HQ in Goose Creek. This location is ideal because it is central to many of our targeted customers and suppliers. The facility is well suited for the heavy metal fabrications that we manufacture.

At W International our focus is to provide innovative engineering solutions, superior products and exceptional customer service. Our success is based on developing reliable, dependable and maintainable processes. We create mutually beneficial partnerships with our customers and suppliers while providing a safe and motivating work environment for our employees.

Our core values start with building a positive team and family spirit. We foster an environment that encourages an open mind that embraces creativity and innovation. We believe in creating win-win partnerships with our customers, suppliers and employees. Growth is accomplished through a strong commitment to quality and amazing customer service.

Our vision is to be the model of excellence in the metal fabrication industry by providing the highest quality products for the Aerospace, Automotive, Commercial, Defense and Energy markets. We also provide innovative solutions and dependable deliveries. We are strongly committed to our customers, suppliers, employees and continuous improvement.

W International was formed by a seasoned leadership team that has been providing exceptional metal fabrications for more than 20 years. Our team has successfully built some of the most complex projects in the Aerospace, Automotive, Commercial, Defense and Energy markets.

Name: MELSİS Elektrik Elektronik Yazılım Donanım Ltd. Şti.
Description:
MELSiS – Mikro Elektronik Sistemler Ltd. Şti. elektronik tasarim ve  haberleşme sistemleri için özgün çözümler üretmektedir. 2012 yılında kurulan MELSiS, Bilkent Cyberparkta faaliyetine devam etmektedir. Elektronik ARGE firmasi olarak kurulan MELSiS, kurulduğu günden bu yana haberleşme sistemleri, karayolu ve demiryolu ulaşım sistemleri alanında faaliyet göstermektedir.

Özgün elektronik tasarım, gömülü sistem yazılımı ve bilgisayar yazılımı geliştiren firmamız  bu konularda yenilikçi çözümler sunarak yurt dışına bağımlılığı azaltıcı projeler yapmaktadır.

MELSiS kurulduğu günden bu yana Elektronik ve Yazılım ARGE konularında adımlar atmıştır. Özellikle ulaşım sistemleri alanında faaliyet göstermektedir. Bu alanda uzman kadrosu ile Karayolu Sinyalizasyonu ve Demiryolu Elektrifikasyonu alanlarında projeler yürütülmektedir. Özellikle Yüksek Hızlı Tren Scada Sistemlerinin Kurulumu ve Bakım konusunda uzmanlaşmış olan MELSiS, bu alanda da yurt dışına olan bağımlılığı azaltmayı hedeflemektedir. MELSiS olarak amacımız Scada sistemlerini uluslararası standartlara uygun olarak milli hale getirmek ve bu alanda ARGE yapmaktır. Karayolu Ulaşım Sistemleri alanında ise MELSiS TSE belgeli kendi ürünleri ile yapım, kurulum ve devreye alma işlerini yapabilmektedir. Bu alanda Karayolları Bölge Müdürlüklerinin ihalelerinde yüklenici firma olarak yer alan MELSiS bu sistemlerin kurulumunu yapmıştır ve halen de yapmaktadır.

Name: STAR Dynamics Corporation
Description:
STAR Dynamics is a world leading manufacturer of radar cross section (RCS) measurement and precision tracking radar systems. Established in 1988, STAR Dynamics was created to meet the needs of America’s Low Observable(LO)/Counter-Low Observable (CLO) programs. Over the past quarter of a century, we have developed and deployed over 100 leading-edge, custom radar systems for both domestic and international businesses and governments.  Today, STAR Dynamics has radar installations around the globe and offices in Ohio, Virginia, Florida and Georgia; and we continue to invest in new product development and innovation for tomorrow’s radar systems.

STAR Dynamics possesses a unique culture of innovation. As a low-volume manufacturer of specialized radar equipment, the core of our company is science and engineering.  Approximately two-thirds of our employees have technical degrees, including the President and CEO, Dr. Jerry Jost. Our Advanced Programs group leads our radar innovation with PhD level physicists and electrical engineers. Our Engineering group develops our radars, providing Electrical, Mechanical, Software, and Systems Engineering. STAR Dynamics is a small, but highly innovative business, and we invest millions of dollars each year into our independent research and development programs.

Name: CROWN PADR Project
Description:
CROWN will lay the foundation for designing and building a next-generation multifunction radiofrequency (RF) system based on Active Electronically Scanned Arrays (AESA) technology for a broad range of applications, to become available in several military domains within the next decade. The project makes a significant contribution towards building European industrial defense capability and ensuring industrial independence. 

The planned activities include R&D assessment and recommendations on critical technologies, R&T activities on system and component level as well as designing, manufacturing and testing of a small-scale prototype. 

CROWN - European active electronically scanned array with Combined Radar, cOmmunications, and electronic Warfare fuNctions for military applications - brings together 11 leading industry members, research centres and an SME from 7 EU countries: Indra (Spain), Thales (France), Office National d'Etudes et de Recherches Aerospatiales - ONERA (France), Hensoldt Sensors (Germany), Fraunhofer Institute for High Frequency Physics and Radar Techniques - FHR (Germany), Saab (Sweden), Totalforsvarets Forskningsinstitut - FOI (Sweden) Nederlandse Organisatie voor Toegepast Natuurwetenschappelijk Onderzoek - TNO (Netherlands), Leonardo (Italy), Elettronica (Italy) and Baltic Institute of Advanced Technology - BPTI (Lithuania). 

CROWN Project has received funding from the European Union’s Preparatory Action on Defence Research under grant agreement No 882407. This presentation reflects only the author’s view. The European Defence Agency and the Commission are not responsible for any use that may be made of the information it contains.

Niron is geared to implement on-budget, on-time turnkey projects, in which its experts accompany and support all development, production and integration stages – from system definition, characterization and engineering, procurement, production, installation and integration, through to factory and acceptance tests, plus Integrated Logistics Support (ILS) that includes documentation, personnel training, assimilation, and ongoing system support.

Name: AISeC - Associazione Italiana Servizio Civile
Description:
AISeC – Associazione Italiana Servizio Civile, è una associazione di promozione sociale che nasce con l’intento di promuovere e difendere l’esistenza e l’utilità del Servizio Civile. Ne fanno parte ex Volontarie e Volontari che credono che l’esperienza del Servizio Civile non vada dispersa e dimenticata, anzi riformata e rivalutata. 

L’idea di associarsi nasce originariamente nell’ambito di tante assemblee nazionale dei giovani delegati e rappresentati in Servizio Civile, la Rappresentanza.Ex Volontarie e Volontari con l’esigenza di avere un “contenitore” all’ interno del quale dare prosecuzione al servizio svolto, chiamati a tutelare ogni giorno il diritto alla difesa della nostra “Patria”, intesa non solo in senso letterale “Italia”, ma come spazio sociale entro il quale operiamo in qualità di Volontarie e Volontari. 

Per fare questo è necessario affrontare e risolvere gli aspetti critici che emergono dall’ attuale legge d’Istituzione del Servizio Civile Nazionale, riconoscendo i cambiamenti intercorsi dal 2001 ad oggi, sfatando quei tabù che non hanno permesso al Servizio Civile di tenere il passo con i cambiamenti, riconoscendo l’energia che invece, si sta sviluppando a livello locale.

A raccolta Civiliste e Civilisti d’Italia, chiamata al ServizioCivile!

AISeC desidera chiamare a raccolta chi ha contribuito negli anni all’affermazione del Servizio Civile, in tutte le sue forme, con le proprie risorse e idee. Ad oggi sappiamo di essere in tanti – oltre 350.000 – ma in pratica non sappiamo dove siamo!

Possiamo costruire la mappa nazionale delle Civiliste e dei Civilisti d’Italia, insieme.

Name: Wolven Industries
Description:
We’re an engineering company that develops technology to serve the following purposes:

--Repair the family
--Protect the family
--Prepare the family

The skills and applications that we’re putting to use to accomplish this mission consist of the following:

--Electrochemical engineering
--Aerospace engineering
--Mechanical engineering
--Software engineering
--Artificial intelligence
--Weapons engineering
--Solar-mechanical engineering

We believe that the earliest version of the family didn’t need any technology whatsoever to come closer together to begin with…and still doesn’t. 

However, with the developing reality that technology is becoming incrementally intertwined with our lives increasingly with every passing day, Wolven Industries seeks to ensure that technology remains beneficial to the human species, by starting with the family. 

We believe that the family is the foundation of any given civilization; break apart the family, and civilization inevitably collapses. Technological advances in computers, software, and social media have aided not in bringing us closer together, but in driving us further apart—even if we’re in the same room. 

But this doesn’t mean that technology in an of itself is evil; we believe that technology is a powerful but neutral tool of human creation that can be used either for or against civilization. If we know for certain that the rise in technology is inevitable, then companies like Wolven Industries can work to positively influence the future, earning a place as one of the guardians of civilization’s wellbeing by keeping your family in mind with everything that we invent, innovate, and thus produce.

Collection, signals analysis, user classification and recognition form the basis of our expertise in preparing demodulation, decoding, content and file extraction solutions.

Our linguists cover more than 200 of the world's languages and still more dialects and our signals analysts cover all HF modulations, Hybrid and Multichannel/VFT modulation schemes. We cover both analog and digital forms of ciphony and encipherments well as legacy modes like Morse Code. Our analysts provide insight into content expectation and cryptanalysis.

There are currently nearly 2000 individual signals on-air in HF -- we track them all. There are more than 70 distinct calling systems on-air today - from Automatic Link Setup (ALIS) and Automatic Link Management (ALM), Automatic Quick Call (AQC) ALE, Link Protected (LP) ALE as well as 1st, 2nd (2G) and 3rd (3G) Generation ALE formats and selective call systems (selcall) - from Codan to Thales Skymaster to Rohde & Schwarz's ALIS and proprietary variants in between- we follow them all!

Our expertise doesn't stop at HF. We provide solutions for every RF spectrum, from ELF to SHF and satellite systems. Airborne, maritime and terrestrial modems as well as the latest datalinks for unmanned vehicles - we have them covered!

---------------

Name: IMEMG The Insensitive Munitions European Manufacturers Group
Description:
IMEMG is a European organisation which assembles leading European armament groups working with IM Technologies
VISION & OBJECTIVES
	Support the development of harmonised international IM policies & regulations;
	Facilitate the development and implementation of harmonised  international IM standards for IM products, during their entire lifecycle;
	Promote and share the benefits of IM technological progress
Aim
Increase the operational benefits for the Armed Forces through the use of Insensitive Munitions

IMEMG Expert Working Groups host a comprehensive range of industry expertise on various issues, such as:
	IM Characterisation ad Qualification
	Ageing Processes
	Hazard Assessment & Classification
	Cost & Benefit Analysis
	Test Procedures
Such groups, launched on either a permanent basis or for a set period of 2 to 3 years, issue recommendations of the evolution of  NATO and UN standards, and account for their work at specific international conferences or IM symposia.
IMEMG encourages different viewpoints to be aired, and aims at forging a common understanding on IM issues.
International conferences, such as EMTWG (formerly IMEMTS), are held in cooperation with the US National Armament Consortium (NAC).
IMEMG also organises IM Days to provide IM European stakeholders with a unique forum, with the support of the European Defence Agency (EDA) and the NATO Munition Safety Information Analysis Center (MSIAC).
IM operational benefits are:
•	Insensitive Munitions are designed to meet operational requirements, while minimising the violence of reaction and collateral damage resulting from accidents or unplanned combat stimuli.
•	IM provides increased safety and survivability, while fulfilling performance requisites.
•	After more than 3 decades of development, the IM concept is acknowledged as inherent in national defence policies. The majority of EU and NATO countries have now implemented IM strategies within their procurement plans

Name: NATO Centre for Maritime Research and Experimentation (CMRE)
Description:
Based in La Spezia, Italy, CMRE is NATO’s world-leading maritime research laboratory, with over 65 years of experience in undersea science and technology.

Originally established during the Cold War to track Soviet submarines, CMRE now tackles today’s maritime threats – from protecting undersea infrastructure and countering sea mines to responding to the resurgence of submarine activity.

Our scientists and engineers develop advanced technologies using artificial intelligence, robotics, and next-generation underwater acoustics. We operate NATO’s first quantum computing lab and its only research vessels – floating laboratories conducting real-world experiments from the Mediterranean to the Arctic.

We work across the NATO Alliance to deepen knowledge from seabed to space and help secure the future of the Alliance at sea.

Name: Southern Launch
Description:
Southern Launch provides bespoke launch and return services in the Southern Hemisphere with end-to-end launch support for our customer's next mission.

Southern Launch offers two unique spaceports in South Australia designed to support different launch and return requirements.

The Whalers Way Orbital Launch Complex is one of the only sites in the world that can provide high-cadence launches to polar and sun-synchronous orbits.

The Koonibba Test Range is the largest commercial rocket testing facility in the Southern Hemisphere and specialises in suborbital launches and returns from space.

We work closely with our customers to find the most cost-effective path to safe, successful, and frequent launch.

Southern Launch takes care of all of the details from regulatory approval to logistics, on-range set up and launch support.

Established in September 2008, it has its presence felt in 2 major cities: Mumbai and New Delhi. The main purpose of Naval Group to be in India would be to support the Indigenisation through ‘Make in India’ activities for Scorpene submarine equipment, to develop the Indian defence eco-system, as well as to develop design services in India with talented Indian engineers.

Naval Group in India has displayed continuous efforts of participating in various defence endeavors for the Indian Navy by empowering Indian industrial partners to cater to the requirements of the Indian shipyards and the Navy. Self-reliance, empowerment and innovation are the driving force for Naval Group in India to better serve the rich Indian coastline.

The thrust to the industrial progress of India can be clearly attributed to the one-of-a-kind initiative called Make in India. India proves to be a strong contender on account of its increasing manufacturing skills.

Strategically significant projects have seen increased participation of indigenous content through a complex process of transfer of technology. Convergence of knowledge, ideas and competence amongst a wide array of cultures introduces an interesting element in the success story of indigenisation.

The endeavor of Naval Group in India is to be a visionary and to further its involvement in empowering more and more industries by creating a robust eco-system that can cater to the varied defence needs of the country.

Name: Naval Undersea Warfare Center Division Keyport
Description:
Naval Undersea Warfare Center Division, Keyport (NUWC Keyport) provides technical leadership, engineering expertise, and unique facility complexes that serve to ensure sustainment of undersea warfare superiority for the United States.  As one of two divisions of the Naval Undersea Warfare Center, Keyport’s mission is focused on developing and applying advanced technical capabilities to test, evaluate, field, and maintain undersea warfare systems and related defense assets. These advanced technical capabilities directly support the full spectrum of Navy undersea programs.

NUWC Keyport is headquartered in the state of Washington on the Puget Sound, about 10 miles west of the city of Seattle and just north of the city of Bremerton. To provide ready support to Fleet operational forces at all major Navy home ports in the Pacific, Keyport also maintains detachments in Southern California and Hawaii, and an operating site in Guam, and an office in Japan. An operating site in Hawthorne, Nevada is used for storage and maintenance of undersea mines. Naval Sea Logistics Center, an echelon five command under Keyport, is located in Pennsylvania and has satellite offices mostly on the East Coast.  In addition, Keyport has a presence in Canada in partnership with the Royal Canadian Navy for undersea range operations. Our people frequently travel around the world to support the U.S. Fleet and Allied militaries.

Keyport’s technical complexes are recognized national assets and include over 2.3 million square feet of specialized technical facilities and nearly 2000 square nautical miles of undersea ranges.  Our highly trained and diverse workforce of engineers, scientists, technicians, and industrial craftsmen are developing, maintaining and providing technical solutions to meet Fleet material and operational readiness needs.  As a part of NAVSEA’s world class team of professionals, we are a responsive, effective, and efficient provider to the Navy.

Name: Navy SBIR Transition Program (Navy STP)
Description: The Navy SBIR Transition Program (Navy STP) supports the successful transition of Navy SBIR/STTR-funded technologies into military acquisition programs, commercial markets, or both. Now in its 25th year, the Navy STP serves as a platform for connecting SBIR/STTR-funded technologies with warfighters, government acquisition and technical personnel, prime contractors, system integrators, and other potential partners and collaborators. This DoN SBIR Program initiative takes a holistic approach to assisting participating small businesses in transitioning their innovative technologies that address the specific needs of the U.S. Navy. Through business mentoring, one-on-one coaching, marketing strategy development, marketing material creation, networking events, and promotional activities, the program provides comprehensive support to help small businesses navigate the complex transition process. Funded by the DoN SBIR Program, all small businesses with active Navy-funded Phase II SBIR/STTR awards are eligible to participate in the program during the first or second year of their Phase II development and demonstration work. Navy STP participating small businesses achieve a 68 percent greater likelihood of transitioning their technology than those that do not participate.
